risk_patterns.description: Without step-in rights, customer cannot maintain service continuity when provider fails.
risk_patterns.impact: No ability to maintain service when provider cannot perform.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical services, check for step-in rights: triggers, scope, duration, and cost allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include step-in rights triggered by: material SLA breach, insolvency, or force majeure. Define scope and cost sharing.

risk_patterns.name: No Force Majeure Termination Right
risk_patterns.trigger: Force majeure clause without termination right for extended events
risk_patterns.description: Without termination right, parties trapped in non-performing contract during extended force majeure.
risk_patterns.impact: Indefinite contract suspension without exit mechanism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check force majeure clause for termination right if event continues beyond defined period (typically 60-90 days).
risk_patterns.remediation: Include termination right if force majeure continues for >60 days, with prorated refund and no termination fees.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: COVID-19 highlighted this issue across many contracts without FM termination rights.

risk_patterns.name: Single-Source Dependency
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical business function dependent on single provider without alternatives
risk_patterns.description: Single-source dependency creates existential risk if provider fails.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete business process failure if sole provider becomes unavailable.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Assess whether service is available from alternative providers. Check for multi-sourcing provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Develop dual-source strategy. Include: right to engage alternative providers, technology escrow, and documented processes.

risk_patterns.name: No Business Continuity Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service without business continuity obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without BCP, provider may have no recovery capability after disruption.
risk_patterns.impact: No recovery from provider-side disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for BCP requirements: plan documentation, RPO/RTO targets, annual testing, and notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require documented BCP with: RPO/RTO targets, annual testing, customer notification of test results, and right to review plan.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Portability on Exit
risk_patterns.trigger: No mechanism for data extraction and migration on contract exit
risk_patterns.description: Without data portability, business data is trapped with incumbent provider.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to migrate to alternative provider due to data lock-in.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data export capability: standard formats, API access, bulk export, and migration assistance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data export in standard formats within 30 days. Provide API access for data migration. Include in transition assistance.

risk_patterns.name: No Pandemic/Extended Disruption Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: Force majeure clause silent on pandemic or extended disruption scenarios
risk_patterns.description: Post-COVID, pandemic exclusion from FM is common but creates coverage gaps for future events.
risk_patterns.impact: No contractual mechanism for managing extended disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if force majeure includes pandemic, epidemic, or public health emergency. Check if excluded post-COVID.
risk_patterns.remediation: Address pandemic explicitly: not excluded from FM, but with mitigation obligations and review mechanism at 30/60/90 days.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Many contracts post-2020 explicitly exclude pandemic from FM, creating coverage gaps.

risk_patterns.name: No Insolvency Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: No provisions addressing counter-party insolvency or financial distress
risk_patterns.description: Insolvency provisions may be unenforceable in some jurisdictions, but absence creates additional risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of service and potentially data if provider becomes insolvent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for insolvency termination rights, financial health monitoring, and protective measures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include: right to terminate on insolvency event (where enforceable), financial health monitoring rights, and data protection measures.

risk_patterns.name: No Alternative Provider Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: No right to engage alternative providers during transition or emergency
risk_patterns.description: Without alternative provider rights, customer is locked to incumbent even during failure.
risk_patterns.impact: No fall-back when primary provider fails.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for right to engage alternative providers: during transition, in emergency, or for specific workstreams.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include right to engage alternative providers: during transition (overlap period), in emergency (step-in), and for discrete workstreams.

risk_patterns.name: Insufficient Termination Notice Period
risk_patterns.trigger: Termination notice period too short for effective transition planning
risk_patterns.description: Short notice periods prevent orderly transition to alternative arrangements.
risk_patterns.impact: Rushed transition causing service disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check termination notice period against realistic transition timeline. Flag if <90 days for complex services.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set notice periods proportional to complexity: simple services 30 days, standard 90 days, complex 180 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Continuity During Dispute
risk_patterns.trigger: No obligation to continue service delivery during contract disputes
risk_patterns.description: Without continuity obligation, provider may suspend services as leverage during disputes.
risk_patterns.impact: Service suspension as bargaining tool during disputes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for obligation to continue service delivery during dispute resolution, provided undisputed fees are paid.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include obligation to maintain service delivery during dispute resolution, with payment of undisputed amounts.

risk_patterns.name: No Geographic Redundancy
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service hosted in single data centre without geographic redundancy
risk_patterns.description: Single-location hosting creates regional disaster risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete service outage from regional event (flood, fire, power grid failure).
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical services, check for geographic redundancy or multi-region hosting requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require primary and secondary data centre in different geographic regions with automatic failover.

risk_patterns.name: No Key Material Escrow
risk_patterns.trigger: Encrypted data with provider-managed keys and no key escrow
risk_patterns.description: If provider loses or destroys encryption keys, customer data is permanently inaccessible.
risk_patterns.impact: Permanent loss of access to encrypted data.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For provider-managed encryption, check for key escrow or customer-managed key options.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require either customer-managed keys (BYOK) or key escrow with independent third party.

risk_patterns.name: No Succession Planning for Critical Roles
risk_patterns.trigger: No succession planning for key provider personnel
risk_patterns.description: Loss of key provider personnel without succession disrupts service delivery.
risk_patterns.impact: Service degradation when key personnel depart.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for succession planning requirements for named key personnel.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require succession plans for all key roles, 30-day notice of departure, and customer approval of replacements.

risk_patterns.name: No Change of Control Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: No notification or termination right on change of control of provider
risk_patterns.description: Provider acquisition may change service quality, strategy, or competitive dynamics.
risk_patterns.impact: Service disruption or competitive conflict from provider acquisition.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for change of control clause: notification requirement, consent right, and termination right.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include: 30-day notification of change of control, right to terminate without penalty within 90 days, and consent requirement for assignment.

risk_patterns.name: No Structured Wind-Down Process
risk_patterns.trigger: Termination clause without structured wind-down period and process
risk_patterns.description: Without structured wind-down, termination creates cliff-edge service discontinuity.
risk_patterns.impact: Abrupt service cessation causing business disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for wind-down schedule covering: parallel running, data migration, knowledge transfer, and documentation handover.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include wind-down schedule: 3-month parallel running, phased handover, documentation delivery, and final acceptance.

risk_patterns.name: No Disaster Recovery Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: DR plan exists but no contractual requirement for regular testing
risk_patterns.description: DR plans degrade over time without regular testing. Untested plans frequently fail.
risk_patterns.impact: DR plan fails when activated due to untested assumptions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for annual DR testing requirement with: customer notification, participation rights, and results reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual DR testing with: 30-day advance notice, customer observation rights, results within 10 days, and remediation plan for gaps.

risk_patterns.name: No API Stability Commitment
risk_patterns.trigger: Integration dependency without API stability or versioning commitments
risk_patterns.description: Breaking API changes without notice disrupt dependent systems and integrations.
risk_patterns.impact: Integration breakage from unannounced API changes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For API-dependent integrations, check for: versioning policy, deprecation notice, backward compatibility commitment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: 12-month deprecation notice, backward-compatible changes only, versioning policy, and migration support.

risk_patterns.name: No Rebate for Extended Outage
risk_patterns.trigger: Extended service outage without prorated fee rebate mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Extended outages should result in fee adjustments beyond service credits.
risk_patterns.impact: Paying full fees during periods of no service.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for prorated rebate mechanism for outages exceeding defined threshold.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include prorated fee rebate for outages exceeding 24 continuous hours in addition to service credits.

risk_patterns.name: No Right to Withhold Disputed Amounts
risk_patterns.trigger: No right to withhold payment for disputed invoice amounts
risk_patterns.description: Without withholding rights, must pay disputed amounts and seek refund later.
risk_patterns.impact: Cash flow disadvantage during billing disputes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for right to withhold disputed portions while paying undisputed amounts.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include right to withhold genuinely disputed amounts (with written notice) while paying undisputed balance.

risk_patterns.name: No Demand Management Process
risk_patterns.trigger: No process for managing and prioritising service requests
risk_patterns.description: Without demand management, resource allocation is reactive and inefficient.
risk_patterns.impact: Uncontrolled demand leading to resource contention and SLA failures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for demand management process with prioritisation criteria and resource allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include demand management process: request classification, priority matrix, and resource allocation rules.

risk_patterns.name: No Asset Management Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Managed service without asset tracking and lifecycle management
risk_patterns.description: Without asset management, hardware and software drift without visibility.
risk_patterns.impact: Untracked assets creating security and compliance blind spots.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For managed services, check for asset inventory and lifecycle management obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require maintained asset inventory, lifecycle management, and quarterly reporting.

risk_patterns.name: No Whistle-Blower Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without provisions for reporting compliance concerns or illegal activity
risk_patterns.description: EU Whistleblower Directive requires protection for persons reporting breaches of EU law.
risk_patterns.impact: Contractual barriers to reporting illegal activity; regulatory non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for whistle-blower protection provisions and right to report to authorities.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clause permitting either party's personnel to report concerns to regulators without breach of confidentiality.

risk_patterns.name: AI Act Non-Compliance in AI Service Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract for AI system without EU AI Act compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: EU AI Act imposes obligations on providers and deployers of AI systems, varying by risk category.
risk_patterns.impact: AI Act fines up to EUR 35M or 7% of global turnover for prohibited practices.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For AI service contracts, check for AI Act risk classification, transparency requirements, and human oversight provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AI Act compliance schedule: risk classification, conformity assessment, transparency obligations, human oversight, and incident reporting.

risk_patterns.name: No Accessibility Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Digital service contract without accessibility compliance (EAA/WCAG)
risk_patterns.description: European Accessibility Act requires digital services to meet accessibility standards.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance and exclusion of users with disabilities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For customer-facing digital services, check for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Include accessibility testing, remediation SLAs, and ongoing compliance monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Sovereignty Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: Government or regulated entity contract without data sovereignty requirements
risk_patterns.description: Government and regulated entities often have strict data sovereignty requirements beyond GDPR.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with sector-specific data sovereignty requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For government and regulated entity contracts, check for sector-specific data sovereignty requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include data sovereignty clause: data stored and processed within specified jurisdiction, no foreign government access provisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Escrow Verification Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: Source code escrow exists but no verification that deposited code is complete and buildable
risk_patterns.description: Unverified escrow deposits may contain incomplete or unbuildable code.
risk_patterns.impact: Escrow release triggered but code is unusable.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for escrow verification testing: build verification, documentation completeness, annual refresh.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual escrow verification: build from source, documentation review, and completeness check by independent agent.

risk_patterns.name: No Customer Data Ownership Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS or cloud contract without explicit customer data ownership confirmation
risk_patterns.description: Without explicit data ownership clause, provider may claim rights over customer data or derivatives.
risk_patterns.impact: Provider claiming ownership or usage rights over customer data.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for explicit clause confirming customer owns all customer data uploaded to or generated within the service.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include explicit clause: customer retains all rights in customer data. Provider granted limited licence only for service provision.

risk_patterns.name: No Multi-Factor Authentication Requirement
risk_patterns.trigger: Access to personal data without MFA requirement
risk_patterns.description: Single-factor authentication is inadequate for systems processing personal data.
risk_patterns.impact: Credential compromise leading to unauthorised data access.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check security requirements for MFA mandate on all systems accessing personal data.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require MFA for all access to systems processing personal data. Hardware tokens for administrative access.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Lineage Tracking
risk_patterns.trigger: Complex data processing without lineage or provenance tracking
risk_patterns.description: Without data lineage, impossible to trace data origins, transformations, and destinations.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to respond to data subject requests or demonstrate compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For complex data processing chains, check for data lineage requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data lineage tracking: origin, transformations, access, and destinations for all personal data.

risk_patterns.name: No Technical Support for Right to Erasure
risk_patterns.trigger: No technical capability to support data erasure requests across all systems
risk_patterns.description: GDPR right to erasure requires complete deletion which may be technically challenging across distributed systems.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to comply with data subject erasure requests.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for technical capability to delete specific personal data across all storage systems including backups.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require technical capability for targeted deletion within 30 days, including backup overwrite within 90 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Warm Standby for Critical Services
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service with cold DR only, no warm standby or active-passive configuration
risk_patterns.description: Cold DR requires hours or days to activate, exceeding RTO for critical services.
risk_patterns.impact: RTO of 4 hours impossible with cold DR requiring 24-48 hours to activate.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical services with RTO <4 hours, verify warm standby or active-passive DR configuration.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require warm standby for critical services: data replication, pre-configured infrastructure, automated failover testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Supply Chain Resilience Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Provider dependent on third-party supply chain without resilience requirements
risk_patterns.description: Provider's supply chain failures cascade to customer without resilience planning.
risk_patterns.impact: Service outage caused by provider's supplier failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Review provider's critical dependencies. Check for supply chain resilience requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: disclosure of critical dependencies, alternative supplier plans, supply chain risk assessment, and notification of disruptions.

risk_patterns.name: No Modern Slavery Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: International supply chain contract without modern slavery provisions
risk_patterns.description: UK Modern Slavery Act and equivalent laws require supply chain due diligence.
risk_patterns.impact: Reputational damage and regulatory non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For international supply chains, check for modern slavery representation and supply chain due diligence.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include modern slavery representation, supply chain audit rights, and termination right for non-compliance.

risk_patterns.name: No Environmental Compliance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: IT services contract without environmental sustainability requirements
risk_patterns.description: CSRD and sustainability regulations increasingly require supply chain environmental reporting.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to report on Scope 3 emissions from IT supply chain.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For significant IT services, check for environmental reporting requirements and energy efficiency commitments.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include sustainability schedule: carbon reporting, energy efficiency targets, renewable energy commitments.

risk_patterns.name: No Media Response Coordination
risk_patterns.trigger: No coordinated approach to media inquiries about the contractual relationship
risk_patterns.description: Uncoordinated media responses can amplify negative publicity and damage both parties.
risk_patterns.impact: Conflicting media statements during joint crisis.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For strategic partnerships, check for media response coordination protocol.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include media coordination clause: mutual notification of inquiries, agreed holding statement, designated spokespersons.

risk_patterns.name: AI Training Data Rights Undefined
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without clear rights over training data and model outputs
risk_patterns.description: Unclear rights over AI training data and model outputs create ownership disputes.
risk_patterns.impact: Dispute over ownership of AI models, training data, and outputs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For AI service contracts, check for: training data ownership, model ownership, output ownership, and usage rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define: customer data not used for training without consent, model ownership per investment, output ownership per contract scope.

risk_patterns.name: No Cross-Tenant Isolation Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-tenant SaaS without regular tenant isolation testing
risk_patterns.description: Multi-tenant isolation can degrade through software updates without regular testing.
risk_patterns.impact: Cross-tenant data leakage undetected for extended periods.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For multi-tenant services, check for annual tenant isolation penetration testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual tenant isolation testing by independent third party with results shared within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Documentation Delivery Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Complex service without documentation requirements for processes and configurations
risk_patterns.description: Without documentation, knowledge is locked in provider's personnel.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to transition service without provider cooperation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for documentation deliverables: runbooks, configuration docs, architecture diagrams, and procedures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require delivery and maintenance of: operations manual, configuration documentation, architecture diagrams, and incident procedures.

risk_patterns.name: No Financial Dispute Escalation Path
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined escalation path for unresolved invoice disputes
risk_patterns.description: Invoice disputes that cannot be resolved at operational level need structured escalation.
risk_patterns.impact: Prolonged billing disputes affecting relationship.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for financial dispute escalation path separate from general dispute resolution.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include financial dispute escalation: operational (10 days), financial controllers (20 days), then CFO-level resolution.

risk_patterns.name: No Record Retention Schedule
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without defined record retention obligations for regulatory compliance
risk_patterns.description: Various regulations require retention of records for specific periods.
risk_patterns.impact: Records destroyed before regulatory retention period expires.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for record retention schedule aligned with applicable regulatory requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define retention schedule for: contract records (6 years), audit records (7 years), personal data (per ROPA), financial records (per local law).

risk_patterns.name: No Problem Management Process
risk_patterns.trigger: Incident management exists but no root cause analysis or problem management process
risk_patterns.description: Without problem management, incidents recur because root causes are not addressed.
risk_patterns.impact: Recurring incidents due to unresolved root causes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for problem management process: RCA requirements, known error database, and permanent fix obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: RCA within 5 business days of P1 incidents, known error database, and permanent fix plan within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Stakeholder Communication Plan During Disruption
risk_patterns.trigger: No defined communication plan for customer notification during service disruptions
risk_patterns.description: Without communication plan, customers learn of outages through their own monitoring or end-user complaints.
risk_patterns.impact: Customer surprised by service disruption with no information on cause or ETA.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident communication plan: notification channels, update frequency, and escalation contacts.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require: initial notification within 15 minutes, hourly updates during P1, status page, and designated communication lead.

risk_patterns.name: No Zero Trust Architecture Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud service without zero trust or micro-segmentation security requirements
risk_patterns.description: Traditional perimeter-based security is inadequate for cloud-native services.
risk_patterns.impact: Lateral movement risk after initial compromise.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For cloud services, check for zero trust architecture: micro-segmentation, identity-based access, continuous verification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require zero trust principles: verify explicitly, least privilege access, assume breach. Micro-segmentation for sensitive data.

risk_patterns.name: No Moral Rights Waiver Attempted
risk_patterns.trigger: Creative deliverables without moral rights waiver or consent to modification
risk_patterns.description: In jurisdictions where moral rights cannot be waived, consent to modifications must be obtained.
risk_patterns.impact: Restriction on modifying creative work without author consent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For creative deliverables, check for moral rights waiver or consent to modification per applicable law.
risk_patterns.remediation: Obtain broadest moral rights waiver permitted. Where waiver impossible, obtain consent to modification and non-attribution.

risk_patterns.name: No Environmental Liability Allocation
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving land, facilities, or industrial processes without environmental liability allocation
risk_patterns.description: Without clear allocation, environmental remediation costs default to the landowner or operator, potentially exposing a party to millions in cleanup costs.
risk_patterns.impact: Unallocated remediation costs averaging EUR 500K-5M for contaminated sites.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving real property, manufacturing, or hazardous materials, check for environmental liability clause and indemnification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include environmental indemnification with baseline assessment, allocation of pre-existing contamination, and ongoing monitoring obligations.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple M&A transactions have resulted in acquirers bearing full remediation costs due to missing environmental allocation.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Hazardous Materials Handling Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Service or construction contract without hazardous materials management provisions
risk_patterns.description: Failure to address hazardous materials handling creates regulatory exposure and potential site contamination liability.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory fines and remediation costs from improper hazardous materials handling.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if contract involves chemicals, waste, or industrial processes. Verify hazardous materials handling requirements are specified.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include hazardous materials management clause: compliance with REACH/CLP regulations, handling procedures, disposal obligations, and incident reporting.

risk_patterns.name: No Carbon Reporting Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply chain contract without Scope 3 emissions reporting requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without supplier carbon reporting, organisations cannot meet CSRD or SEC climate disclosure obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to comply with mandatory climate disclosure regulations; potential greenwashing liability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For material suppliers, check for carbon reporting obligations including Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions data.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual GHG emissions reporting per GHG Protocol, science-based reduction targets, and audit rights on environmental data.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Companies subject to CSRD face penalties for incomplete Scope 3 reporting.

risk_patterns.name: Missing Waste Management Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract generating waste streams without waste management provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without waste management obligations, improper disposal creates regulatory liability and reputational risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties and cleanup costs from improper waste disposal.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving manufacturing, construction, or IT equipment, check for waste management and circular economy provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include waste management clause: classification, segregation, licensed disposal, duty of care certificates, and WEEE compliance for electronics.

risk_patterns.name: No Sustainability Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply contract with environmental commitments but no verification mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Environmental claims without audit rights are unverifiable and may constitute greenwashing.
risk_patterns.impact: Greenwashing exposure and inability to verify supply chain environmental compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if environmental commitments include audit rights, third-party certification requirements, or verification mechanisms.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include sustainability audit rights: annual self-assessment, biennial third-party audit, corrective action plans, and termination right for material non-compliance.

risk_patterns.name: No Deforestation-Free Commitment
risk_patterns.trigger: Agricultural or commodity supply contract without deforestation-free sourcing commitment
risk_patterns.description: EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) requires due diligence on deforestation-free supply chains for specified commodities.
risk_patterns.impact: Import prohibition and fines under EUDR for non-compliant commodities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving palm oil, soy, beef, coffee, cocoa, rubber, or wood, check for deforestation-free commitments and geolocation data requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include deforestation-free commitment with geolocation data, traceability systems, and compliance with EUDR due diligence requirements.

risk_patterns.name: No Water Usage Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving water-intensive operations without water management provisions
risk_patterns.description: Water-intensive operations without management provisions create environmental risk and regulatory exposure in water-stressed regions.
risk_patterns.impact: Operational disruption from water scarcity; regulatory penalties for excessive water usage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For data centres, manufacturing, or agricultural contracts, check for water usage monitoring, reduction targets, and discharge standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include water management clause: usage monitoring, reduction targets, discharge compliance, and water stress assessment for facility locations.

risk_patterns.name: No Circular Economy Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Procurement contract for physical goods without end-of-life or recyclability requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without circular economy provisions, organisations bear disposal costs and miss sustainability targets.
risk_patterns.impact: Increased disposal costs and inability to meet corporate sustainability commitments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For hardware, equipment, or materials procurement, check for take-back schemes, recyclability requirements, and extended producer responsibility.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include circular economy clause: minimum recycled content, take-back obligations, design for recyclability, and EPR compliance.

risk_patterns.name: No Environmental Pollution Insurance
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving potential pollution risk without environmental impairment liability insurance
risk_patterns.description: Standard general liability policies typically exclude gradual pollution, leaving environmental cleanup costs uninsured.
risk_patterns.impact: Uninsured remediation costs potentially exceeding contract value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For construction, manufacturing, or chemical handling contracts, check for environmental impairment liability insurance requirement.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require environmental impairment liability insurance with minimum coverage of EUR 5M per occurrence, covering both sudden and gradual pollution.

risk_patterns.name: No Biodiversity Impact Assessment
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction or land-use contract without biodiversity impact requirements
risk_patterns.description: Projects affecting natural habitats without biodiversity assessment risk regulatory delays and reputational damage.
risk_patterns.impact: Project delays from regulatory intervention; biodiversity net loss liability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For land-use, construction, or infrastructure projects, check for biodiversity impact assessment and net gain requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require pre-construction biodiversity assessment, mitigation hierarchy compliance, and biodiversity net gain of at least 10%.

risk_patterns.name: No Energy Efficiency Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Data centre or facility management contract without energy efficiency targets
risk_patterns.description: Without energy efficiency requirements, operational costs escalate and carbon reduction targets are missed.
risk_patterns.impact: Higher operational costs and inability to meet carbon reduction commitments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For data centre or facility contracts, check for PUE targets, energy efficiency improvement obligations, and renewable energy sourcing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include energy efficiency clause: PUE target of 1.3 or below, annual improvement obligations, and minimum 50% renewable energy sourcing.

risk_patterns.name: Uncapped Environmental Remediation Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Environmental remediation obligations without financial cap or time limit
risk_patterns.description: Open-ended remediation obligations can persist for decades with escalating costs.
risk_patterns.impact: Multi-decade remediation obligations potentially exceeding EUR 10M.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if environmental remediation obligations have financial caps, time limits, and defined completion standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap environmental remediation at agreed amount with defined cleanup standards, monitoring period, and regulatory sign-off as completion trigger.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Several industrial site acquisitions have resulted in remediation costs exceeding the purchase price.

risk_patterns.name: No Climate Risk Disclosure
risk_patterns.trigger: Investment or lending agreement without climate-related financial risk disclosures
risk_patterns.description: TCFD/ISSB-aligned climate risk disclosures are increasingly mandatory for financial agreements.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with mandatory climate disclosure frameworks; stranded asset risk.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial agreements, check for climate risk disclosure requirements aligned with TCFD or ISSB standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual TCFD-aligned climate risk disclosures covering governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics/targets.

risk_patterns.name: No Conflict Minerals Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Electronics or manufacturing supply contract without conflict minerals due diligence
risk_patterns.description: EU Conflict Minerals Regulation requires due diligence on tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold sourcing.
risk_patterns.impact: Import restrictions and regulatory penalties for non-compliant mineral sourcing.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving 3TG minerals (tin, tantalum, tungsten, gold), check for conflict minerals due diligence and reporting obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include conflict minerals clause: OECD due diligence compliance, smelter/refiner identification, annual reporting, and audit rights.

risk_patterns.name: No REACH Chemical Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Product supply contract without REACH/RoHS chemical compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: Products containing restricted substances face import bans and recall obligations under REACH and RoHS.
risk_patterns.impact: Product recalls, import bans, and regulatory fines for restricted substance violations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For product supply contracts, check for REACH registration, SVHC declarations, RoHS compliance, and substance restriction lists.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require REACH compliance declaration, SVHC notification, RoHS certificates, and indemnification for restricted substance violations.

risk_patterns.name: No Green Claims Substantiation
risk_patterns.trigger: Marketing or supply contract with environmental claims lacking substantiation requirements
risk_patterns.description: Unsubstantiated green claims constitute greenwashing under the EU Green Claims Directive.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties and reputational damage from greenwashing allegations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if any environmental marketing claims are backed by third-party verification, lifecycle assessments, or recognised certifications.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require third-party certification for all environmental claims, lifecycle assessment data, and compliance with EU Green Claims Directive.

risk_patterns.name: No Scope 3 Reduction Targets in Supply Contracts
risk_patterns.trigger: Major supply contract without emission reduction commitments aligned to science-based targets
risk_patterns.description: Without supplier emission reduction targets, organisations cannot achieve their own net-zero commitments.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to achieve corporate net-zero targets; investor and regulatory scrutiny.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For material suppliers, check for science-based emission reduction targets, annual reporting, and progressive improvement requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require SBTi-aligned reduction targets, annual emissions reporting, and progressive improvement with contractual consequences for non-achievement.

risk_patterns.name: No Packaging and Waste Regulation Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Product distribution contract without packaging waste regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation imposes minimum recycled content and recyclability requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with packaging regulations; product withdrawal risk.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For product supply or distribution contracts, check for packaging recyclability, recycled content, and EPR compliance requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include packaging compliance clause: minimum recycled content, recyclability design, EPR registration, and labelling requirements.

risk_patterns.name: No Environmental Incident Reporting
risk_patterns.trigger: Operations contract without environmental incident notification and reporting obligations
risk_patterns.description: Delayed environmental incident reporting can escalate contamination and regulatory penalties.
risk_patterns.impact: Escalated contamination from delayed response; regulatory penalties for late notification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for environmental incident notification timeframes, reporting requirements, and remediation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require immediate notification (within 4 hours) of environmental incidents, root cause analysis within 30 days, and remediation plan approval.

risk_patterns.name: No Land Contamination Baseline Assessment
risk_patterns.trigger: Property lease or acquisition without pre-existing contamination assessment
risk_patterns.description: Without baseline assessment, tenant or buyer may become liable for pre-existing contamination.
risk_patterns.impact: Liability for pre-existing contamination costing EUR 100K-5M in remediation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For property transactions, check for Phase I/II environmental site assessment requirements and baseline condition reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require Phase I environmental assessment before completion; Phase II if contamination suspected. Include indemnity for pre-existing contamination.

risk_patterns.name: No Anti-Bribery and Corruption Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: International or government-adjacent contract without anti-bribery provisions
risk_patterns.description: UK Bribery Act, FCPA, and EU anti-corruption directives require adequate procedures including contractual controls.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution, unlimited fines, and debarment from public contracts.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for anti-bribery representations, compliance obligations, audit rights, and termination triggers for violations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ABC clause: zero-tolerance representation, compliance with UK Bribery Act/FCPA, audit rights, and immediate termination for breach.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple multinationals have faced billion-dollar FCPA settlements due to supply chain bribery.

risk_patterns.name: No Sanctions Screening Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Cross-border contract without sanctions compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: Failure to screen counterparties against sanctions lists creates criminal liability exposure.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal penalties, asset freezing, and reputational destruction from sanctions violations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for sanctions compliance representation, screening obligations, and ongoing monitoring requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include sanctions clause: initial and ongoing screening against OFAC/EU/UN lists, representation of non-restricted status, and immediate termination for breach.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Banks have paid multi-billion dollar fines for sanctions violations facilitated through inadequate contractual controls.

risk_patterns.name: No Export Control Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Technology or defence-related contract without export control provisions
risk_patterns.description: Export of controlled technology without proper classifications and licenses is a criminal offence.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution, export privilege denial, and substantial fines.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For technology contracts, check for ECCN/EAR classifications, end-use restrictions, re-export prohibitions, and deemed export controls.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include export control clause: classification obligations, end-use certificates, re-export restrictions, deemed export acknowledgment, and compliance audit rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Modern Slavery Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply chain contract without modern slavery due diligence requirements
risk_patterns.description: UK Modern Slavery Act and EU CSDDD require supply chain due diligence on forced labour.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties, supply chain disruption, and severe reputational damage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For supply contracts, check for modern slavery representations, supply chain transparency, audit rights, and remediation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include modern slavery clause: zero-tolerance representation, supply chain mapping, annual disclosure, worker grievance mechanisms, and audit rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Protection Compliance Representation
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving personal data processing without GDPR compliance representations
risk_patterns.description: Without compliance representations, joint controller liability may expose both parties to GDPR fines.
risk_patterns.impact: Joint and several liability for GDPR violations up to 4% of global turnover.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data protection compliance representations, processing basis documentation, and DPA references.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include GDPR compliance representation, require Article 28 DPA as schedule, and define processing instructions clearly.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory Change Adaptation Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-year contract without mechanism for adapting to regulatory changes
risk_patterns.description: Regulatory landscape evolves during long-term contracts; without adaptation mechanisms, compliance gaps emerge.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with new regulations; contract renegotiation disputes.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >2 years, check for regulatory change clause covering notification, assessment, cost allocation, and adaptation process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include regulatory change mechanism: 30-day notification of material changes, joint impact assessment, cost sharing for compliance, and adjustment process.

risk_patterns.name: No AML/KYC Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services or high-value contract without anti-money laundering provisions
risk_patterns.description: AML regulations require customer due diligence and ongoing monitoring for regulated activities.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal liability, regulatory sanctions, and licence revocation for AML failures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial services, real estate, or high-value contracts, check for AML/KYC obligations, suspicious activity reporting, and record-keeping.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AML clause: KYC verification, beneficial ownership disclosure, suspicious activity reporting cooperation, and record retention for 5 years.

risk_patterns.name: No Whistleblower Protection Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without whistleblower reporting and protection mechanisms
risk_patterns.description: EU Whistleblower Directive requires internal reporting channels and protection from retaliation.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties and inability to detect compliance violations early.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for whistleblower reporting channel references, non-retaliation commitments, and investigation cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include whistleblower clause: reporting channel availability, non-retaliation commitment, investigation cooperation, and confidentiality protection.

risk_patterns.name: No Tax Compliance Representation
risk_patterns.trigger: International contract without tax compliance representations or transfer pricing provisions
risk_patterns.description: Tax non-compliance by counterparty can create secondary liability and reputational risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Secondary tax liability, withholding obligations, and reputational damage from association with tax avoidance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For international contracts, check for tax compliance representation, transfer pricing documentation, and anti-avoidance warranties.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include tax compliance representation, require transfer pricing documentation, and include indemnity for secondary tax liabilities.

risk_patterns.name: No Accessibility Compliance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Software or digital service contract without accessibility standards requirements
risk_patterns.description: European Accessibility Act (EAA) requires digital products and services to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards from June 2025.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties and inability to serve customers with disabilities; EAA fines.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For digital service contracts, check for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance requirements, testing obligations, and remediation timeframes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, annual accessibility audits, VPAT documentation, and remediation of critical issues within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: No AI Act Compliance Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system procurement or deployment contract without EU AI Act compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: EU AI Act imposes risk-based obligations on AI providers and deployers effective August 2025.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to EUR 35M or 7% of global turnover for non-compliance with EU AI Act.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For AI-related contracts, check for risk classification, transparency requirements, conformity assessment obligations, and human oversight provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AI Act compliance clause: risk classification, conformity assessment, transparency documentation, human oversight mechanisms, and incident reporting.

risk_patterns.name: No Consumer Protection Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: B2C or platform contract without consumer protection regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: EU Consumer Rights Directive and Digital Content Directive impose mandatory consumer protections that cannot be contracted away.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory enforcement, class actions, and void contract terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For B2C contracts, check for cooling-off period, information requirements, conformity guarantees, and unfair terms review.
risk_patterns.remediation: Ensure compliance with Consumer Rights Directive, Digital Content Directive, and Unfair Contract Terms Directive. Include required pre-contractual information.

risk_patterns.name: No Competition Law Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Distribution, licensing, or partnership agreement without competition law safeguards
risk_patterns.description: Vertical agreements with anti-competitive provisions are void and subject to fines under Art. 101 TFEU.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to 10% of global turnover; void contract provisions; private damages claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for resale price maintenance, exclusive territories, non-compete duration >5 years, and information exchange provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Review against Vertical Block Exemption Regulation. Remove hardcore restrictions, limit non-compete to 5 years, and ensure market share thresholds are met.

risk_patterns.name: No Product Safety Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Product supply contract without EU General Product Safety Regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: EU GPSR requires economic operators to ensure products placed on the market are safe.
risk_patterns.impact: Product recalls, market withdrawal, and regulatory fines for unsafe products.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For product supply contracts, check for GPSR compliance, CE marking, technical documentation, and recall cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include product safety clause: GPSR compliance representation, CE marking, technical file availability, recall cooperation, and product liability indemnification.

risk_patterns.name: No Industry Certification Maintenance
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract requiring specific certifications (ISO, SOC2, PCI DSS) without maintenance obligations
risk_patterns.description: Certifications lapse without ongoing maintenance, creating compliance gaps during contract term.
risk_patterns.impact: Compliance gaps when required certifications lapse; regulatory audit failures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if required certifications have maintenance obligations, notification of lapses, and re-certification timelines.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require ongoing certification maintenance, 30-day notification of lapses or failures, re-certification within 90 days, and termination right if not restored.

risk_patterns.name: No Record Retention Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without record retention obligations aligned to regulatory requirements
risk_patterns.description: Multiple regulations require specific record retention periods; failure to retain creates evidence gaps.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to produce records during regulatory investigations or litigation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for record retention clause specifying types, periods, formats, and destruction procedures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include record retention clause: 7-year general retention, specific regulatory requirements, secure storage, and controlled destruction.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory Insurance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Regulated activity contract without mandatory insurance coverage
risk_patterns.description: Many regulated activities require specific insurance types and minimum coverage levels.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance and uninsured liability exposure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if the activity requires mandatory insurance (professional indemnity, employers liability, public liability) and verify minimum coverage levels.
risk_patterns.remediation: Specify all mandatory insurance types, minimum coverage levels, A-rated insurer requirement, and annual certificate of insurance provision.

risk_patterns.name: No Beneficial Ownership Disclosure
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract with entity of unclear ownership structure without beneficial ownership disclosure
risk_patterns.description: AML and sanctions regulations require knowledge of beneficial ownership to prevent money laundering.
risk_patterns.impact: AML violations, sanctions exposure, and regulatory penalties.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for beneficial ownership disclosure, UBO declaration, and ongoing notification of ownership changes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require beneficial ownership disclosure to 25% threshold, annual re-confirmation, and notification of changes within 14 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Labour Law Compliance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Outsourcing or staffing contract without labour law compliance obligations
risk_patterns.description: Joint employer liability and TUPE obligations can transfer to the contracting party if labour compliance is not addressed.
risk_patterns.impact: Joint employer liability, TUPE transfer obligations, and employment tribunal claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For outsourcing or staffing contracts, check for labour law compliance, TUPE provisions, minimum wage warranties, and working time compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include labour compliance clause: minimum wage warranty, working time compliance, TUPE cooperation, and indemnity for employment claims.

risk_patterns.name: No Health and Safety Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving on-site work without health and safety provisions
risk_patterns.description: Principal contractors bear statutory health and safety duties that cannot be fully delegated.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution, HSE enforcement, and personal injury liability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For on-site work contracts, check for H&S method statements, risk assessments, incident reporting, and competency requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include H&S clause: method statements before work, risk assessments, incident reporting within 24 hours, and right to stop unsafe work.

risk_patterns.name: No Cross-Border Data Transfer Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving data transfer outside EEA without adequate transfer mechanism
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Chapter V requires an adequate transfer mechanism for personal data leaving the EEA.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR fines up to 4% of global turnover; data transfer injunctions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if personal data leaves the EEA. If so, verify adequacy decision, SCCs, BCRs, or other Chapter V mechanism is in place.
risk_patterns.remediation: Implement appropriate transfer mechanism: SCCs (module 2 for C2P), transfer impact assessment, and supplementary measures where needed.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Schrems II invalidated Privacy Shield; organisations without alternative mechanisms faced enforcement.

risk_patterns.name: No Digital Services Act Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Platform or intermediary service contract without DSA compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: EU Digital Services Act imposes transparency, content moderation, and due diligence obligations on digital service providers.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to 6% of global turnover for DSA non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For platform or hosting service contracts, check for DSA compliance obligations, transparency reporting, and notice-and-action mechanisms.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include DSA compliance clause: transparency reporting, notice-and-action procedures, designated contact point, and content moderation cooperation.

risk_patterns.name: No Digital Markets Act Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract with gatekeeper platform without DMA compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: EU Digital Markets Act imposes obligations on gatekeepers regarding interoperability, data access, and fair dealing.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to 10% of global turnover; contractual terms that conflict with DMA are void.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts with designated gatekeeper platforms, check for DMA compliance, data portability, and interoperability provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include DMA-awareness clause: gatekeeper obligation compliance, data portability rights, and review trigger if gatekeeper designation changes.

risk_patterns.name: No Cybersecurity Certification Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical infrastructure or essential service contract without cybersecurity certification requirements
risk_patterns.description: NIS2 Directive requires essential entities to implement certified cybersecurity measures.
risk_patterns.impact: NIS2 non-compliance fines up to EUR 10M or 2% of global turnover.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts with essential or important entities under NIS2, check for cybersecurity certification requirements (ISO 27001, SOC 2, or EU scheme).
risk_patterns.remediation: Require ISO 27001 or equivalent certification, annual penetration testing, incident response capability, and supply chain security measures.

risk_patterns.name: No Financial Reporting and Audit Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Joint venture or partnership agreement without financial reporting and audit provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without financial reporting obligations, partners lack visibility into financial performance and potential mismanagement.
risk_patterns.impact: Financial mismanagement undetected; inability to verify profit sharing or earn-out calculations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For JV, partnership, or earn-out agreements, check for financial reporting requirements, audit rights, and accounting standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require monthly management accounts, annual audited financials, agreed accounting policies, and independent audit rights at partner expense.

risk_patterns.name: No Insider Trading Controls
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract providing access to material non-public information without insider trading controls
risk_patterns.description: Access to MNPI without trading controls creates market abuse regulation exposure.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution under Market Abuse Regulation; unlimited fines.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For M&A, investment, or advisory contracts involving listed companies, check for insider trading restrictions and information barrier requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include MAR compliance clause: insider list maintenance, trading restrictions, information barriers, and MNPI handling procedures.

risk_patterns.name: No PCI DSS Compliance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Payment processing contract without PCI DSS compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: Any entity storing, processing, or transmitting cardholder data must comply with PCI DSS.
risk_patterns.impact: Card scheme fines, breach liability, and loss of payment processing capability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving payment card data, check for PCI DSS compliance level, AOC requirements, and breach liability allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require current PCI DSS compliance at appropriate level, annual AOC provision, breach notification within 24 hours, and forensic investigation cooperation.

risk_patterns.name: No HIPAA/Healthcare Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare-related contract involving PHI without HIPAA business associate agreement
risk_patterns.description: HIPAA requires Business Associate Agreements for any entity handling Protected Health Information.
risk_patterns.impact: HIPAA penalties up to USD 1.9M per violation category; OCR enforcement actions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts involving US healthcare data, check for BAA, PHI safeguards, breach notification, and minimum necessary standard compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Execute HIPAA BAA as contract schedule: PHI safeguards, 60-day breach notification, sub-contractor flow-down, and termination on material breach.

risk_patterns.name: No Sector-Specific Licensing Verification
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract for regulated services without verification of required licences and authorisations
risk_patterns.description: Many sectors require specific licences; contracting with unlicensed parties voids agreements and creates liability.
risk_patterns.impact: Void contracts, regulatory penalties, and unenforceable service commitments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if the services require specific licences (FCA, SRA, CQC, OFCOM). Verify licence representation and maintenance obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require licence representation, evidence of current authorisation, notification of any changes, and termination right if licence revoked.

risk_patterns.name: No Trade Compliance Programme
risk_patterns.trigger: International trade contract without comprehensive trade compliance programme requirements
risk_patterns.description: International trade involves overlapping export controls, sanctions, customs, and anti-corruption requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: Multi-jurisdictional regulatory violations; export privilege denial; criminal prosecution.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For international trade contracts, check for trade compliance programme requirements covering export controls, sanctions, customs, and anti-corruption.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require documented trade compliance programme: screening procedures, classification, licence management, record keeping, and training.

risk_patterns.name: No ESG Reporting Alignment
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply contract without alignment to customer ESG reporting frameworks (CSRD, ESRS)
risk_patterns.description: CSRD requires value chain ESG data that depends on supplier cooperation and aligned reporting.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to complete mandatory CSRD disclosures; qualified ESG audit reports.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For material suppliers, check for ESRS-aligned data provision, double materiality assessment cooperation, and ESG audit support.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ESG reporting clause: ESRS-aligned data provision, materiality assessment cooperation, data quality requirements, and annual ESG disclosure.

risk_patterns.name: No Cyber Resilience Act Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Software or connected product supply contract without EU Cyber Resilience Act provisions
risk_patterns.description: EU CRA requires manufacturers of products with digital elements to ensure cybersecurity throughout the product lifecycle.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to EUR 15M or 2.5% of global turnover; product withdrawal from EU market.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For software or IoT product contracts, check for CRA compliance, vulnerability handling, security updates, and SBOM requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include CRA compliance clause: secure-by-design obligations, vulnerability disclosure, 5-year security update commitment, SBOM provision, and CE marking.

risk_patterns.name: No DORA Compliance in Financial Services Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: ICT services contract with financial entity lacking DORA-mandated provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires specific contractual provisions for all ICT service arrangements of financial entities.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance fines; contract voidability; supervisory intervention.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For ICT contracts with banks, insurers, or investment firms, check for DORA Article 30 mandatory provisions including exit strategy, audit rights, and incident reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include all DORA Article 30 mandatory provisions: service description, data location, exit strategy, audit rights, incident reporting, subcontracting controls.

risk_patterns.name: No Entire Agreement Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without entire agreement clause to exclude prior representations
risk_patterns.description: Without entire agreement clause, pre-contractual representations may form part of the agreement, creating unintended obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Liability for pre-contractual statements and sales representations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for entire agreement clause excluding prior representations, with carve-out for fraud.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include entire agreement clause: excludes prior representations and negotiations, with express carve-out for fraudulent misrepresentation.

risk_patterns.name: No Severability Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without severability provision to preserve remaining terms if one is held invalid
risk_patterns.description: Without severability, invalidity of one provision may void the entire agreement.
risk_patterns.impact: Entire contract voided due to single invalid provision.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for severability clause with blue-pencil or replacement provision mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include severability clause: invalid provisions severed, remaining terms continue, parties negotiate replacement in good faith.

risk_patterns.name: No Waiver Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without non-waiver provision protecting against implied waiver of rights
risk_patterns.description: Without non-waiver clause, failure to enforce a right may be treated as permanent waiver.
risk_patterns.impact: Permanent loss of contractual rights through implied waiver.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for non-waiver clause requiring written waiver for any right to be waived.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include non-waiver clause: no waiver unless in writing, failure to enforce not a waiver, waiver of one breach not waiver of future breaches.

risk_patterns.name: No Notices Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without formal notice provisions for communications under the agreement
risk_patterns.description: Without notice provisions, critical communications (termination, breach) may not be properly delivered or evidenced.
risk_patterns.impact: Invalid termination or breach notices; disputed receipt of critical communications.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for notices clause specifying addresses, methods (registered post, email), and deemed delivery rules.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include notices clause: specified addresses, permitted methods (registered post + email), deemed delivery times, and update notification obligations.

risk_patterns.name: No Governing Law Specified
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without express governing law clause
risk_patterns.description: Without governing law clause, applicable law is determined by conflict-of-laws rules, creating uncertainty.
risk_patterns.impact: Uncertainty over which law governs; expensive preliminary litigation to determine jurisdiction.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for express governing law clause specifying both substantive law and procedural rules.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include governing law clause specifying applicable law, exclusion of renvoi/conflict rules, and agreed jurisdiction for disputes.

risk_patterns.name: No Dispute Resolution Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without defined dispute resolution procedure
risk_patterns.description: Without dispute resolution mechanism, any disagreement defaults to litigation in potentially uncertain jurisdiction.
risk_patterns.impact: Expensive and time-consuming litigation; jurisdictional uncertainty.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for tiered dispute resolution: negotiation, mediation, then arbitration or litigation with agreed venue.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include tiered dispute resolution: 30-day good-faith negotiation, mediation (60 days), then binding arbitration or agreed court jurisdiction.

risk_patterns.name: Ambiguous Scope of Work
risk_patterns.trigger: Service or deliverable description is vague, subjective, or references undefined external documents
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous scope creates disputes over what is included and enables scope creep or underdelivery.
risk_patterns.impact: Scope disputes, budget overruns, and delivery failures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if scope is described with specificity: deliverables, acceptance criteria, exclusions, and assumptions clearly stated.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define scope with specific deliverables, measurable acceptance criteria, explicit exclusions, stated assumptions, and change control process.

risk_patterns.name: No Force Majeure Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without force majeure provisions for events beyond party control
risk_patterns.description: Without force majeure, parties remain liable for non-performance regardless of extraordinary circumstances.
risk_patterns.impact: Liability for non-performance during pandemic, war, or natural disaster events.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for force majeure clause with defined events, notification requirements, mitigation duties, and termination right.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include force majeure clause: defined events including pandemic, cyber-attack; notice within 48 hours; mitigation duty; termination after 90 days.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: COVID-19 exposed widespread inadequacy of force majeure provisions across supply chains.

risk_patterns.name: No Contractual Limitation Period
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without express limitation period for bringing claims
risk_patterns.description: Without agreed limitation period, statutory periods apply which may be longer and vary by jurisdiction.
risk_patterns.impact: Exposure to claims years after contract completion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for contractual limitation period for claims, typically 12-24 months from delivery or termination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 12-24 month contractual limitation period from date of breach or termination, with carve-outs for fraud and latent defects.

risk_patterns.name: No Counterparts or Electronic Signature Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without counterparts clause enabling execution in multiple copies or electronically
risk_patterns.description: Without counterparts clause, parties may dispute validity of separately executed or electronically signed copies.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed contract validity due to execution formalities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for counterparts clause permitting execution in separate copies and electronic signature.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include counterparts clause: any number of counterparts, each an original, electronic signatures have same effect as wet-ink.

risk_patterns.name: No Survival Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without survival clause specifying which obligations continue after termination
risk_patterns.description: Without survival clause, important obligations like confidentiality and IP ownership may cease on termination.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of confidentiality protection and IP rights after contract ends.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for survival clause listing provisions that survive termination: confidentiality, IP, liability, indemnification, dispute resolution.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include survival clause: confidentiality (3-5 years), IP ownership (perpetual), liability caps (2 years), indemnification, and dispute resolution survive termination.

risk_patterns.name: No Third-Party Rights Exclusion
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without exclusion of third-party enforcement rights
risk_patterns.description: Under Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999, third parties may acquire enforceable rights if not excluded.
risk_patterns.impact: Unintended third-party claims under the contract.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for express exclusion of third-party rights, with any intended third-party beneficiaries expressly identified.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include third-party rights exclusion under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999, with express exceptions if needed.

risk_patterns.name: No Good Faith Dealing Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term relational contract without express good faith obligation
risk_patterns.description: Without good faith obligation, parties may act opportunistically within the letter but against the spirit of the agreement.
risk_patterns.impact: Opportunistic behaviour undermining the commercial relationship.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For long-term or relational contracts, check for good faith dealing obligation with defined scope.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include good faith clause with defined scope: cooperation, information sharing, not acting to frustrate the other party's interests.

risk_patterns.name: Unclear or Missing Definitions
risk_patterns.trigger: Key contractual terms used without clear definitions
risk_patterns.description: Undefined terms lead to interpretation disputes, particularly for 'material', 'reasonable', and 'commercially reasonable'.
risk_patterns.impact: Interpretation disputes and unintended obligations from undefined terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if key terms are defined: 'Material', 'Confidential Information', 'Intellectual Property', 'Services', 'Deliverables', 'Data'.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include comprehensive definitions section covering all key terms. Define 'material' and 'reasonable' with objective criteria where possible.

risk_patterns.name: No Order of Precedence
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-document contract without order of precedence clause resolving conflicts
risk_patterns.description: Without precedence rules, conflicts between main agreement, schedules, and SoWs create ambiguity.
risk_patterns.impact: Contradictory terms with no mechanism to determine which prevails.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts with multiple schedules or incorporated documents, check for order of precedence clause.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include order of precedence: 1) amendments, 2) main agreement, 3) DPA, 4) SLA, 5) SoW, 6) general terms. Higher number yields to lower.

risk_patterns.name: Unilateral Amendment Right
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract granting one party the right to amend terms unilaterally
risk_patterns.description: Unilateral amendment rights allow one party to change the deal after execution, undermining certainty.
risk_patterns.impact: Material terms changed without consent; effective loss of contractual certainty.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for any provisions allowing unilateral changes to terms, pricing, scope, or SLAs. Flag 'may update' or 'reserves the right to modify' language.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require mutual written consent for all amendments. If operational changes needed, limit to non-material terms with 30-day notice and opt-out right.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Common in cloud service agreements where vendors reserve the right to change terms via website updates.

risk_patterns.name: No Assignment Restriction
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without restriction on assignment to third parties
risk_patterns.description: Without assignment restrictions, counterparty can transfer obligations to less capable or less creditworthy entities.
risk_patterns.impact: Contract obligations transferred to unknown or less capable third party.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for anti-assignment clause requiring written consent, with common exceptions for group company assignments.
risk_patterns.remediation: Restrict assignment without prior written consent, with exception for group companies. Include novation requirements for permitted assignments.

risk_patterns.name: No Change of Control Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Strategic contract without change of control notification or termination rights
risk_patterns.description: Acquisition by competitor or less capable owner changes the commercial basis without contractual protection.
risk_patterns.impact: Competitor gains access to confidential information or critical services.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for change of control clause covering direct and indirect ownership changes, with notification and termination rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include change of control clause: 30-day advance notification, right to terminate within 90 days of change, and data return obligations.

risk_patterns.name: No Termination for Convenience Right
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term contract without right to terminate for convenience with notice
risk_patterns.description: Without termination for convenience, party is locked in for full term regardless of changed circumstances.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to exit underperforming or unnecessary contracts.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for termination for convenience with reasonable notice period and reasonable wind-down costs.
