risk_patterns.remediation: Include termination for convenience with 90-day notice, payment for work completed, reasonable wind-down costs, and transition assistance.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Termination for Cause Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract with narrow or unclear termination for cause triggers
risk_patterns.description: Narrow termination rights prevent exit even when counterparty materially breaches or becomes insolvent.
risk_patterns.impact: Trapped in contract with materially breaching counterparty.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check termination triggers cover: material breach, insolvency, change of control, regulatory non-compliance, and persistent minor breaches.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include comprehensive termination triggers: material breach with 30-day cure, insolvency, change of control, regulatory breach, and repeated minor breaches.

risk_patterns.name: No Indemnification Procedure
risk_patterns.trigger: Indemnification clause without notification and defence procedures
risk_patterns.description: Without procedures, indemnifying party may deny liability due to late notification or loss of control over defence.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of indemnification rights due to procedural non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if indemnification includes: prompt notification, defence control rights, settlement approval, and cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include indemnification procedure: notify within 10 business days, indemnifying party controls defence, no settlement without consent, cooperation duty.

risk_patterns.name: No Confidentiality Exceptions
risk_patterns.trigger: Confidentiality clause without standard exceptions for prior knowledge, public domain, and legal disclosure
risk_patterns.description: Overbroad confidentiality without exceptions restricts legitimate use of information and creates inadvertent breach risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Inadvertent breach liability for information that is publicly available or independently developed.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for standard confidentiality exceptions: prior knowledge, public domain, independent development, third-party disclosure, and legal compulsion.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include standard exceptions: already known, publicly available, independently developed, received from third party, and legally compelled disclosure.

risk_patterns.name: Unclear IP Ownership
risk_patterns.trigger: Development contract without clear intellectual property ownership allocation
risk_patterns.description: Without clear IP allocation, ownership defaults to the creator, potentially leaving the commissioning party without rights.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of IP rights in commissioned work; inability to use or modify deliverables.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for express IP ownership clause distinguishing background IP, foreground IP, and improvements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include IP clause: customer owns foreground IP, supplier retains background IP with perpetual licence, improvements to background IP licensed back.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Many disputes arise from consultant-developed software where IP ownership was not contractually assigned.

risk_patterns.name: No Warranty Period
risk_patterns.trigger: Deliverable contract without defined warranty period for defect correction
risk_patterns.description: Without warranty period, buyer has no contractual right to defect correction after acceptance.
risk_patterns.impact: No remedy for latent defects discovered after acceptance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for warranty period: typically 12 months from acceptance, covering defects in materials and workmanship.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 12-month warranty period from acceptance: defect correction at no cost, response times for critical defects, and warranty extension for repairs.

risk_patterns.name: Representations Do Not Survive Completion
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A or investment agreement where representations do not survive closing
risk_patterns.description: If representations expire at completion, buyer has no post-closing remedy for pre-existing issues.
risk_patterns.impact: No warranty claims for pre-existing issues discovered after completion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For M&A agreements, check if warranties survive completion and for how long (typically 18-24 months; tax warranties 7 years).
risk_patterns.remediation: Warranties survive completion: general 18-24 months, tax 7 years, title perpetual. Include de minimis, basket, and cap mechanisms.

risk_patterns.name: No Contractual Insurance Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Service or construction contract without mandatory insurance requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without insurance obligations, counterparty's liability is only as good as their financial position.
risk_patterns.impact: Unrecoverable losses when counterparty lacks assets to honour claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for insurance requirements: types (PI, GL, EL, cyber), minimum coverage, A-rated insurer, and annual certificate provision.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require professional indemnity (2x contract value), general liability (EUR 5M), employers liability (statutory), and cyber insurance (EUR 2M).

risk_patterns.name: No Data Return on Termination
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without data return or destruction obligations on termination
risk_patterns.description: Without data return provisions, customer loses access to their data when the contract ends.
risk_patterns.impact: Permanent loss of business-critical data on contract termination.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data return clause: format, timeframe, verification, and secure destruction certificate.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include data return clause: export in standard format within 30 days, verification period, secure destruction after 90 days with certificate.

risk_patterns.name: No Subcontracting Controls
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract allowing unlimited subcontracting without approval or flow-down requirements
risk_patterns.description: Uncontrolled subcontracting dilutes accountability and may introduce unvetted parties into the service chain.
risk_patterns.impact: Service quality degradation; security risks from unvetted subcontractors.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for subcontracting clause: prior approval required, flow-down of obligations, right to object, and primary contractor remains liable.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require prior written approval for subcontractors, flow-down of all material obligations, right to reject, and primary contractor retains full liability.

risk_patterns.name: Automatic Renewal Without Opt-Out Notice
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract auto-renews without adequate opt-out notice period or clear renewal terms
risk_patterns.description: Auto-renewal without notice locks parties into extended terms they may not have intended.
risk_patterns.impact: Unintended multi-year commitment through missed opt-out window.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for auto-renewal clause: renewal term, notice period for opt-out (should be 60-90 days), and renewal pricing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 90-day opt-out notice period, renewal reminder obligation, and renewal pricing agreed or capped before renewal date.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Very common in SaaS contracts where customers miss narrow opt-out windows.

risk_patterns.name: No Step-In Rights for Critical Services
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service contract without customer step-in rights during provider failure
risk_patterns.description: Without step-in rights, customer has no mechanism to maintain service continuity during provider failure.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete service outage during provider distress with no intervention mechanism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical services, check for step-in rights: trigger events, scope of step-in, cost allocation, and duration.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include step-in rights: triggered by material failure or insolvency, access to systems and premises, at provider cost, for up to 6 months.

risk_patterns.name: No Non-Solicitation of Personnel
risk_patterns.trigger: Consulting or outsourcing contract without non-solicitation of key personnel
risk_patterns.description: Without non-solicitation, counterparty can recruit key staff, disrupting operations.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of key personnel to counterparty during or after engagement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for mutual non-solicitation clause covering key personnel for 12 months post-termination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual non-solicitation: 12 months post-termination, covers direct and indirect solicitation, with recruitment fee exception.

risk_patterns.name: No General Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Outsourced service contract without customer audit rights
risk_patterns.description: Without audit rights, customer cannot verify compliance with contractual obligations, security standards, or regulatory requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to verify service delivery, security posture, or regulatory compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for audit rights clause: scope, frequency, notice period, cost allocation, and access to records and premises.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual audit rights with 30-day notice, access to relevant records and personnel, and additional audit rights for security incidents.

risk_patterns.name: No Liability Cap
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without any limitation on aggregate liability
risk_patterns.description: Without a liability cap, financial exposure is unlimited, creating existential risk from a single contract.
risk_patterns.impact: Potentially unlimited financial exposure from a single contractual relationship.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for aggregate liability cap, typically expressed as a multiple of annual fees (1-2x) or a fixed amount.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual liability cap: 12-24 months of fees for direct damages, with super-cap carve-outs for IP, data breach, and confidentiality at 2-5x.

risk_patterns.name: SaaS Usage-Based Overcharge Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract with usage-based pricing and no metering transparency or dispute mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Opaque usage metering allows vendors to overcharge without customer ability to verify consumption data.
risk_patterns.impact: Systematic overcharging of 10-30% on usage-based fees.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for metering methodology, customer access to usage dashboards, audit rights on metering data, and dispute mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require real-time usage dashboard access, monthly detailed usage reports, independent metering audit rights, and credit for overcharges.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Common in cloud IaaS and API-based SaaS where metering methodologies are vendor-controlled.

risk_patterns.name: SaaS Data Extraction Cost Trap
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract where data export incurs egress fees or requires paid professional services
risk_patterns.description: Hidden data extraction costs create financial barrier to switching providers.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected costs of EUR 10K-100K for data extraction during provider transition.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data export provisions: included in subscription, egress fees, format availability, and extraction timeline.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include data export at no additional cost, standard format (CSV/JSON/API), 90-day extraction window post-termination, and technical assistance.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Variation Cost Overrun
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without caps on variation costs or pre-pricing requirements
risk_patterns.description: Uncontrolled variation orders can double project costs without proper cost control mechanisms.
risk_patterns.impact: Project cost overruns of 50-200% from uncontrolled variations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for variation order process: pre-pricing requirement, approval thresholds, aggregate cap, and daywork rates.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require pre-priced variations with written approval, aggregate variation cap of 15% of contract sum, and fixed daywork rates in schedule.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Edinburgh tram project and many large infrastructure projects saw massive overruns from variation accumulation.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Retention Release Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract with retention funds held without clear release conditions or bond alternative
risk_patterns.description: Retention funds (typically 5%) are routinely withheld beyond contractual release dates, damaging subcontractor cash flow.
risk_patterns.impact: Cash flow strain from 5-10% of contract value held indefinitely.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check retention clause: percentage (standard 5%), release triggers, timeframes, and retention bond alternative.
risk_patterns.remediation: Limit retention to 3%, release 50% at practical completion and balance at end of defects period, or accept retention bond alternative.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: UK government has proposed abolishing retention in construction; widespread non-release is a systemic industry issue.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Earn-Out Manipulation Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A agreement with earn-out provisions without anti-manipulation protections
risk_patterns.description: Without protections, acquirer can manipulate earn-out metrics through accounting changes, cost allocation, or operational decisions.
risk_patterns.impact: Earn-out value reduced by 50-100% through legitimate but manipulative operational decisions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for earn-out protections: consistent accounting policies, ordinary course of business obligations, anti-manipulation covenants, and dispute mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include earn-out protections: locked accounting policies, ordinary course covenants, no material changes without consent, independent accountant dispute resolution.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Earn-out disputes are among the most common post-M&A litigation.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Completion Accounts Dispute Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A agreement with completion accounts mechanism but without clear accounting policies or dispute process
risk_patterns.description: Completion accounts adjustments are a major source of post-closing disputes due to accounting policy ambiguities.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed price adjustments of 5-20% of transaction value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for completion accounts: defined accounting policies, worked example, expert determination, and timeframes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include detailed accounting policies, worked example in schedule, 60-day preparation period, 30-day review, and independent expert determination.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Warranty Claim Deductible Too High
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A warranty claim mechanism with excessive de minimis or basket thresholds
risk_patterns.description: High deductibles effectively eliminate warranty protection for all but the largest claims.
risk_patterns.impact: No recovery for warranty breaches below the basket threshold.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check de minimis (typically 0.1% of price) and basket (typically 1% of price). Flag if basket exceeds 2% or operates as a true deductible.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set de minimis at 0.1% and tipping basket at 1% of purchase price. Use tipping basket (not deductible) so full amount is recoverable once threshold met.

risk_patterns.name: Excessive Severance/Golden Parachute
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract with severance exceeding 12 months or uncapped change-of-control payments
risk_patterns.description: Excessive severance packages create disproportionate financial exposure on termination.
risk_patterns.impact: Severance costs exceeding EUR 500K-5M per executive.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check severance provisions: multiple of salary, bonus inclusion, benefit continuation, and change-of-control triggers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap severance at 12 months base salary, exclude discretionary bonus, limit benefit continuation to 6 months, and require mitigation duty.

risk_patterns.name: Consulting T&M Cost Overrun
risk_patterns.trigger: Consulting engagement on time-and-materials basis without budget cap or cost controls
risk_patterns.description: Open-ended T&M engagements without controls routinely exceed initial estimates by 2-5x.
risk_patterns.impact: Budget overruns of 100-400% above initial estimate.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For T&M contracts, check for: not-to-exceed amount, budget notification thresholds, burn rate reporting, and stop-work rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include not-to-exceed cap with 80% notification threshold, weekly burn rate reporting, monthly budget reviews, and customer stop-work right.

risk_patterns.name: No IP Royalty Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Royalty-bearing licence without audit rights on royalty calculations
risk_patterns.description: Without audit rights, licensor cannot verify accuracy of royalty statements and payments.
risk_patterns.impact: Systematic under-reporting of royalties estimated at 10-25% of actual usage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For royalty-bearing licences, check for audit rights, record retention, underpayment interest, and audit cost allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual audit rights at licensor cost, unless underpayment exceeds 5% (then licensee pays). 3-year record retention and interest on underpayments.

risk_patterns.name: Government Contract Clawback Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract without clear gainshare/painshare boundaries or clawback limits
risk_patterns.description: Government contracts often include broad clawback rights that can recover substantial amounts post-delivery.
risk_patterns.impact: Post-delivery clawback of 10-30% of contract value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for gainshare/painshare mechanism, clawback triggers, caps on recovery, and dispute process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap painshare at 10% of contract value, require 12-month limitation period for clawback, and include independent verification of savings calculations.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare Volume Guarantee Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare supply contract with minimum volume guarantees based on unrealistic demand forecasts
risk_patterns.description: Volume guarantees in healthcare are particularly risky due to formulary changes, generic substitution, and regulatory changes.
risk_patterns.impact: Take-or-pay obligations for unused healthcare products or services.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check volume commitment basis, adjustment mechanisms for formulary changes, and force majeure including regulatory changes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Base volume on rolling 12-month history, include +/-25% flexibility band, and allow adjustment for formulary changes and regulatory events.

risk_patterns.name: AI/ML Compute Cost Escalation
risk_patterns.trigger: AI/ML service contract with usage-based GPU/inference pricing and no cost controls
risk_patterns.description: AI compute costs can escalate exponentially as usage grows, with GPU pricing volatile and opaque.
risk_patterns.impact: AI compute costs exceeding initial estimates by 5-20x during scaling.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for compute cost caps, committed use discounts, pricing transparency, and right to move workloads.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include monthly compute cost cap, committed use discounts for baseline, pricing transparency on GPU hours, and portability rights for model weights.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple organisations have experienced 10x+ compute cost increases when AI workloads scaled.

risk_patterns.name: AI Training Data Copyright Liability
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without indemnification for training data copyright infringement
risk_patterns.description: AI models trained on copyrighted data expose users to secondary copyright infringement claims.
risk_patterns.impact: Class action copyright claims; injunctive relief preventing AI model use.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for training data provenance warranties, copyright indemnification, and model output IP allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require training data provenance disclosure, IP indemnification for model outputs, and commitment to use licensed/public domain training data.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple lawsuits filed against AI companies for training data copyright infringement (NYT v. OpenAI, Getty v. Stability AI).

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Performance Bond Coverage
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract with performance bond below 10% of contract value or from low-rated surety
risk_patterns.description: Inadequate bond coverage leaves employer exposed to contractor default costs exceeding the bond value.
risk_patterns.impact: Shortfall between bond value and actual completion costs on contractor default.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check bond value (minimum 10% of contract sum), surety rating (minimum A), and bond conditions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require on-demand performance bond at 10% of contract sum from A-rated surety, with automatic reinstatement on partial draw.

risk_patterns.name: Regulatory Capital Impact Not Assessed
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services contract without assessment of regulatory capital implications
risk_patterns.description: Certain contractual arrangements can increase regulatory capital requirements, affecting profitability.
risk_patterns.impact: Increased capital requirements reducing return on equity by 2-5%.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial services contracts, check if capital impact assessment has been performed and reflected in pricing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require regulatory capital impact assessment before execution, reflect capital costs in pricing, and include capital relief optimisation provisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Foreign Exchange Hedging Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-year international contract without FX hedging requirements for material currency exposures
risk_patterns.description: Unhedged currency exposures on long-term contracts can materially erode margins.
risk_patterns.impact: Margin erosion of 10-20% from adverse FX movements over multi-year terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >EUR 1M with payments in different currencies, check for FX hedging requirements or rate adjustment mechanisms.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include FX clause: specified payment currency, hedging obligation for exposures >EUR 500K, annual rate review, and adjustment mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: Government Late Payment Exposure
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract without adequate late payment protections despite public sector payment legislation
risk_patterns.description: Government entities frequently pay late despite statutory obligations, creating cash flow strain.
risk_patterns.impact: Cash flow strain from 60-120 day actual payment cycles despite 30-day contractual terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for late payment interest (Late Payment of Commercial Debts Act), prompt payment obligations, and escalation mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include statutory late payment interest, 30-day payment obligation, monthly payment cycle, and escalation to procurement authority for persistent late payment.

risk_patterns.name: IP Licence Fee Structure Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: IP licence with fee structure that becomes disproportionate as business scales
risk_patterns.description: Per-unit or revenue-based licence fees can become economically unviable at scale without renegotiation rights.
risk_patterns.impact: Licence costs becoming disproportionate to value at scale.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check licence fee structure for scalability: volume discounts, caps, renegotiation triggers, and benchmarking rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include volume discount tiers, annual fee cap, renegotiation right if fees exceed 5% of revenue, and benchmarking against market rates.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Locked Box Leakage Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Locked box M&A mechanism without comprehensive leakage protections
risk_patterns.description: Between locked box date and completion, value can leak from the target through dividends, management fees, or related party transactions.
risk_patterns.impact: Value leakage of 2-10% between locked box date and completion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for comprehensive permitted leakage definition, non-permitted leakage covenants, and EUR-for-EUR purchase price reduction for breaches.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define permitted leakage exhaustively, covenant against non-permitted leakage, require monthly leakage certificates, and EUR-for-EUR price reduction for breaches.

risk_patterns.name: No Credit Support for Counterparty Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: High-value contract with financially weak counterparty and no credit support mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Without credit support, counterparty insolvency leaves claims as unsecured debts in administration.
risk_patterns.impact: Total loss of delivered value and ongoing service if counterparty becomes insolvent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >EUR 500K, assess counterparty credit risk. Check for credit support: parent guarantee, letter of credit, escrow, or retention.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require credit support proportionate to exposure: parent guarantee for group companies, LC for unrated entities, or retention fund for ongoing services.

risk_patterns.name: No Professional Fee Recovery in Construction Dispute
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without professional fee recovery provisions in dispute resolution
risk_patterns.description: Construction disputes require expert witnesses, quantity surveyors, and legal counsel, often costing 10-20% of the disputed amount.
risk_patterns.impact: Professional fees of EUR 50K-500K in construction disputes with no recovery mechanism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check dispute resolution clause for costs allocation, professional fee recovery, and interim payment preservation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include loser-pays cost allocation for professional fees, interim payment preservation during disputes, and adjudication for interim binding decisions.

risk_patterns.name: SaaS True-Up Cost Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract with annual true-up for licence usage above committed levels
risk_patterns.description: True-up clauses can result in large retroactive invoices when actual usage exceeds committed levels.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected true-up invoices of 20-50% above annual committed spend.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for true-up mechanism: frequency, overage rates, notification thresholds, and right to reduce before true-up.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include monthly usage alerts at 80% threshold, quarterly true-up instead of annual, overage rate no more than 110% of list price, and right to optimise.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Enterprise SaaS vendors commonly use annual true-ups to generate significant additional revenue.

risk_patterns.name: AI Output Liability Not Allocated
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without allocation of liability for AI-generated outputs that cause harm
risk_patterns.description: AI outputs can cause financial, reputational, or physical harm; liability allocation between provider and user is essential.
risk_patterns.impact: Unlimited liability exposure for harm caused by AI-generated outputs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for AI output liability allocation, fitness-for-purpose disclaimers, human-in-the-loop requirements, and indemnification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate AI output liability based on control: provider liable for model defects, user liable for misuse. Include human oversight requirements and output validation.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare Regulatory Price Change Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare supply contract without regulatory price change adjustment mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Government pricing interventions in healthcare can materially change contract economics without adjustment mechanism.
risk_patterns.impact: Margin elimination from regulatory price reductions without contractual adjustment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For healthcare supply contracts, check for regulatory price change mechanism, reference pricing adjustments, and margin protection.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include regulatory price change clause: automatic adjustment for mandatory price changes, margin protection floor, and termination right if uneconomic.

risk_patterns.name: Stock Option Vesting Acceleration Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment agreement with full vesting acceleration on change of control without performance conditions
risk_patterns.description: Full acceleration vesting on CoC creates significant financial exposure during M&A transactions.
risk_patterns.impact: Accelerated vesting costs of 5-20% of transaction value for key employees.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for single-trigger vs double-trigger acceleration, scope of acceleration, and tax gross-up obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Use double-trigger acceleration (CoC + termination), limit to pro-rata acceleration, and no tax gross-up for parachute payments.

risk_patterns.name: No Insurance Subrogation Waiver
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without mutual waiver of subrogation rights to prevent insurer recovery actions
risk_patterns.description: Without subrogation waiver, insurers can pursue recovery claims against the other contracting party, undermining the relationship.
risk_patterns.impact: Post-incident insurer subrogation claims disrupting commercial relationship.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for mutual waiver of subrogation rights, particularly for property damage and business interruption insurance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include mutual waiver of subrogation for property damage and BI insurance, requiring each party to obtain waiver endorsement from their insurer.

risk_patterns.name: Government Budget Cancellation Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract without compensation for budget-driven cancellation
risk_patterns.description: Government contracts are subject to budget appropriation; cancellation without compensation creates stranded cost risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Unrecoverable investment in bid costs, mobilisation, and contract-specific resources.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for budget cancellation provisions: compensation for sunk costs, demobilisation costs, and minimum notice period.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include budget cancellation clause: recovery of committed costs, demobilisation payment, minimum 6-month notice, and no cancellation in first 12 months.

risk_patterns.name: Data Processing Hidden Cost Exposure
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA with broadly scoped processing that enables processor to charge for compliance activities
risk_patterns.description: Broad processing scope in DPAs creates hidden costs for DSARs, breach response, audits, and DPIAs.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected compliance costs of EUR 10K-50K annually for DSAR, audit, and DPIA support.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if DPA obligations are costed: DSAR assistance, audit support, breach investigation, and DPIA cooperation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include reasonable number of DSARs, audits, and DPIAs in base price. Define additional assistance rates. Cap DSAR response costs.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Insurance Gap Between Parties
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without joint names insurance or clear allocation of insurable risk periods
risk_patterns.description: Gaps between contractor and employer insurance coverage create uninsured risk periods during construction.
risk_patterns.impact: Uninsured losses from events during coverage gaps.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for joint names CAR insurance, clear handover of insurable interest, and no coverage gaps between contractor and employer policies.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require joint names CAR insurance from contractor, employer risk insurance from employer, and no gap between contractor completion and employer policy inception.

risk_patterns.name: AI Model Deprecation Cost Exposure
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract allowing provider to deprecate models without migration support or timeline
risk_patterns.description: AI model deprecation without migration support forces costly retraining, fine-tuning, or application redesign.
risk_patterns.impact: Forced migration costs of EUR 50K-500K when AI models are deprecated.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for model deprecation provisions: minimum support period, advance notice, migration assistance, and parallel running.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require 12-month advance notice of model deprecation, 6-month parallel running, migration assistance at no additional cost, and API compatibility.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: OpenAI model deprecations have forced enterprises to rewrite prompt engineering and fine-tuning.

risk_patterns.name: No Contract Value Indexation
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-year contract with fixed pricing and no indexation mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Fixed pricing over multiple years erodes real value for the supplier and may lead to service degradation.
risk_patterns.impact: 10-25% real-value erosion over a 3-5 year term in high-inflation environments.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >2 years, check for annual price indexation tied to CPI or relevant industry index.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual CPI indexation with cap at 5%, effective on anniversary, with 90-day advance notice of adjusted pricing.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Warranty & Indemnity Insurance Gaps
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction relying on W&I insurance without understanding policy exclusions
risk_patterns.description: W&I insurance policies contain significant exclusions that may leave material warranty breaches uncovered.
risk_patterns.impact: Material warranty breaches falling within W&I policy exclusions, leaving buyer unprotected.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Review W&I policy exclusions: known issues, forward-looking warranties, transfer pricing, pension, and environmental typically excluded.
risk_patterns.remediation: Map warranty coverage against W&I exclusions, negotiate bespoke coverage for material risks, and maintain direct seller recourse for excluded items.

risk_patterns.name: Unenforceable Training Repayment Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract with training repayment clause that may be unenforceable as penalty
risk_patterns.description: Training repayment clauses that do not diminish over time or are disproportionate may be unenforceable as penalties.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to recover training investment of EUR 5K-50K per employee.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check training repayment: proportionate to actual cost, diminishing over 12-24 months, and not a penalty clause.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set repayment at actual cost, declining monthly over 24 months, with clear documentation of training costs and employee acknowledgment.

risk_patterns.name: Consulting Success Fee Ambiguity
risk_patterns.trigger: Consulting engagement with success fee tied to vaguely defined outcomes
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous success fee triggers lead to disputes over whether outcomes were achieved and fee is owed.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed success fees of EUR 50K-500K due to ambiguous achievement criteria.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check success fee triggers for objectivity: measurable criteria, verification method, payment timeline, and dispute resolution.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define success criteria with objective, measurable thresholds, independent verification method, and 30-day payment after verified achievement.

risk_patterns.name: No Minimum Commitment Below Cost
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract with minimum commitment that does not cover the provider's fixed costs
risk_patterns.description: Minimum commitments below the provider's fixed cost base create loss-making contracts.
risk_patterns.impact: Operating at a loss on minimum commitment with no adjustment mechanism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For providers: check if minimum commitment covers fixed costs of service delivery including dedicated staff and infrastructure.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set minimum commitment to cover at least 80% of fixed costs, with annual recalibration and adjustment for scope changes.

risk_patterns.name: No Milestone Payment Protection for Deliverables
risk_patterns.trigger: Project contract with milestone payments but no protection for work completed if project is cancelled
risk_patterns.description: Milestone-only payment creates cash flow risk for providers who invest in work between milestones.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of work-in-progress value between milestones if project is cancelled.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for protection of work-in-progress: interim progress payments, WIP recovery on termination, and milestone advance provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include monthly progress payments against milestones, WIP recovery on termination at cost plus reasonable margin, and 20% advance on milestone start.

risk_patterns.name: SaaS API Deprecation Without Notice
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract allowing API changes or deprecation without adequate notice or backward compatibility
risk_patterns.description: API breaking changes without notice disrupt integrations and force unplanned development work.
risk_patterns.impact: Unplanned integration rework costing EUR 10K-100K per breaking change.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for API stability commitments: minimum support period, deprecation notice, backward compatibility, and versioning policy.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require 12-month API deprecation notice, 6-month backward compatibility, semantic versioning, and migration documentation.

risk_patterns.name: SaaS Multi-Tenancy Isolation Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract without data isolation guarantees in multi-tenant environment
risk_patterns.description: Inadequate tenant isolation can lead to data leakage, noisy-neighbour performance issues, and cross-tenant attacks.
risk_patterns.impact: Data exposure to other tenants; performance degradation from neighbour activity.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for tenant isolation guarantees: logical/physical separation, performance isolation, encryption per tenant, and isolation testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require documented isolation architecture, per-tenant encryption keys, performance isolation guarantees, and annual isolation testing results.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Programme Without Float or Contingency
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract with unrealistic programme and no float or contingency provisions
risk_patterns.description: Construction programmes without float lead to delay claims from the first disruption event.
risk_patterns.impact: Systemic delay claims from first programme disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check construction programme for float (minimum 10%), contingency periods, and extension of time mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require programme with minimum 10% float, defined critical path, monthly progress reporting, and clear extension of time mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Site Access Dependencies
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without clear site access obligations and consequence of restricted access
risk_patterns.description: Site access delays by the employer disrupt the construction programme without clear remedy mechanism.
risk_patterns.impact: Programme delays and standing time costs from restricted site access.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for site access obligations: dates, conditions, employer responsibilities, and contractor entitlements for restricted access.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define specific access dates, employer site readiness obligations, contractor entitlement for delays (time and cost), and alternative access provisions.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Post-Completion Integration Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A agreement without transition services or integration planning provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without transition services, acquired business may lose critical operational capabilities on day one.
risk_patterns.impact: Operational disruption from loss of shared services, IT systems, or key personnel on completion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for transition services agreement, integration planning obligations, and day-one readiness provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include TSA for minimum 12 months covering IT, HR, finance, and operations. Define integration milestones, cost allocation, and exit criteria.

risk_patterns.name: Unenforceable Employment Restrictive Covenants
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract with overly broad restrictive covenants that may be unenforceable
risk_patterns.description: Overly broad non-compete and non-solicit covenants are routinely struck down, leaving employer unprotected.
risk_patterns.impact: No protection from competitive activity when covenants are struck down.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check restrictive covenants: duration (max 12 months), geographic scope (reasonable), activity scope (proportionate), and consideration.
risk_patterns.remediation: Limit non-compete to 6-12 months, defined geographic area, specific competitive activities, and include garden leave as consideration.

risk_patterns.name: AI Hallucination/Accuracy Risk Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without accuracy guarantees, hallucination rate SLAs, or human oversight requirements
risk_patterns.description: AI systems produce hallucinated or inaccurate outputs; without accuracy SLAs, outputs may be relied upon incorrectly.
risk_patterns.impact: Business decisions based on inaccurate AI outputs; legal liability for AI-generated errors.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for accuracy SLAs, hallucination rate commitments, ground truth validation, human-in-the-loop requirements, and output disclaimers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include accuracy SLA with defined metrics, maximum hallucination rate, mandatory human review for high-stakes decisions, and output provenance.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Legal professionals sanctioned for citing AI-hallucinated case law in court filings.

risk_patterns.name: AI Model Version Drift Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract allowing model updates without customer testing or approval
risk_patterns.description: Model updates can change behaviour, break fine-tuning, and alter output quality without customer awareness.
risk_patterns.impact: Degraded output quality, broken workflows, and regression in fine-tuned performance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for model versioning: version pinning rights, update notification, testing period, and rollback capability.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require version pinning, 30-day advance notice of updates, customer testing period, rollback capability, and change log documentation.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: GPT-4 behaviour changes between versions caused significant disruption to production applications.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare Clinical Governance Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare IT contract without clinical governance and patient safety requirements
risk_patterns.description: Healthcare IT systems without clinical governance can contribute to patient safety incidents.
risk_patterns.impact: Patient safety incidents; regulatory enforcement; clinical negligence claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For healthcare IT contracts, check for clinical governance provisions, clinical safety case, and medical device classification assessment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clinical governance clause: clinical safety case (DCB0129/DCB0160), incident reporting, clinical review board, and patient safety escalation.

risk_patterns.name: Government Security Clearance Dependency
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract requiring security-cleared personnel without clearance maintenance provisions
risk_patterns.description: Security clearance lapses or revocation can render personnel unable to deliver, with 6-12 month replacement timelines.
risk_patterns.impact: Service disruption from clearance issues with 6-12 month replacement timeline.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clearance requirements, maintenance obligations, notification of changes, and substitute personnel provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require clearance maintenance, 5-day notification of changes, pre-cleared substitute pool, and 30-day replacement timeline.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Migration Testing Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: System implementation contract without data migration testing and validation requirements
risk_patterns.description: Data migration without testing leads to data loss, corruption, and extended parallel running.
risk_patterns.impact: Data loss or corruption affecting business continuity; extended parallel running costs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data migration plan: trial migration, validation criteria, parallel running, and rollback provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data migration plan with trial migration, 100% record count validation, data integrity checks, parallel running, and rollback capability.

risk_patterns.name: Consulting Knowledge Dependency Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Consulting engagement creating critical dependency on consultant knowledge without knowledge transfer
risk_patterns.description: Consulting engagements that do not include knowledge transfer create ongoing dependency and recurring costs.
risk_patterns.impact: Ongoing dependency on consultant at premium rates; lost knowledge on engagement end.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for knowledge transfer obligations: documentation, training sessions, shadow period, and completion criteria.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include knowledge transfer as deliverable: documentation, 2-week shadow period, training sessions for internal team, and completion assessment.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Design Liability Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Design and build contract with unclear allocation of design liability between employer and contractor
risk_patterns.description: Ambiguous design liability allocation leads to gaps where neither party accepts responsibility for design defects.
risk_patterns.impact: Unallocated design defect liability resulting in costly remedial works.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clear design liability allocation, fitness for purpose vs reasonable skill and care, and professional indemnity insurance backing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Clearly allocate design liability with PI insurance backing, distinguish between contractor design (fitness for purpose) and employer design (information accuracy).

risk_patterns.name: Open Source Supply Chain Risk in Software Delivery
risk_patterns.trigger: Software delivery contract without SBOM requirements or open source licence compliance
risk_patterns.description: Undocumented open source dependencies create licensing compliance risk and vulnerability exposure.
risk_patterns.impact: GPL contamination of proprietary code; unpatched vulnerabilities in dependencies.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for SBOM delivery obligation, open source licence compliance, vulnerability management for dependencies, and licence compatibility review.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require SBOM (CycloneDX/SPDX format) delivery, open source policy compliance, no copyleft in proprietary deliverables, and quarterly vulnerability reports.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Log4j vulnerability highlighted risks of undocumented open source dependencies across supply chains.

risk_patterns.name: Financial Services Operational Resilience Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services ICT contract without operational resilience testing and recovery requirements
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires financial entities to test operational resilience of critical ICT services annually.
risk_patterns.impact: DORA non-compliance; inability to demonstrate operational resilience to regulators.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For financial services ICT contracts, check for DORA resilience testing, recovery objectives, exit planning, and annual testing obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include DORA-compliant resilience provisions: annual testing, defined RTO/RPO, exit strategy, scenario testing participation, and results reporting.

risk_patterns.name: AI Data Residency Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without data residency guarantees for training data and inference
risk_patterns.description: AI services may process data across jurisdictions for training and inference, violating data residency requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR violations from cross-border processing; data sovereignty breaches.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data residency guarantees covering input data, model training, inference processing, and output storage locations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require EU data residency for all processing stages, documented data flow map, and contractual prohibition on cross-border transfers without consent.

risk_patterns.name: Government FOI Disclosure Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract without FOI protection for commercially sensitive information
risk_patterns.description: Government contracts are subject to FOI disclosure; without protection, commercially sensitive information may be disclosed.
risk_patterns.impact: Disclosure of pricing, methodology, and commercially sensitive information to competitors via FOI.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for FOI clause: supplier consultation before disclosure, marking of confidential information, and exemption arguments.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include FOI clause: mark confidential information, 5-day consultation before disclosure, and supply exemption arguments for commercially sensitive material.

risk_patterns.name: TUPE Transfer Risk in Outsourcing
risk_patterns.trigger: Outsourcing contract without clear TUPE provisions for staff transfer
risk_patterns.description: TUPE transfers employment to the new provider; without clear provisions, liabilities are disputed.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed employment liabilities and unfair dismissal claims on service transfer.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For outsourcing contracts, check for TUPE provisions: employee information sharing, liability allocation, and ongoing terms protection.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include TUPE clause: employee liability information (ELI) sharing 28 days before transfer, indemnity for pre-transfer liabilities, and terms protection.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Transition Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: Outsourced service contract without defined transition-in and transition-out plans
risk_patterns.description: Without transition plans, service changes result in extended disruption and quality degradation.
risk_patterns.impact: 3-6 month service disruption during unplanned transitions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for transition-in plan (milestones, knowledge transfer, parallel running) and transition-out plan (data migration, cooperation, timeline).
risk_patterns.remediation: Include transition-in plan with 90-day parallel running and transition-out plan with 12-month assistance, data migration, and documentation.

risk_patterns.name: Ambiguous Practical Completion Definition
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract with unclear definition of practical completion or handover criteria
risk_patterns.description: Disputed practical completion delays possession, rent commencement, and defects liability period start.
risk_patterns.impact: Delayed occupation and revenue from disputed completion status.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clear practical completion definition, snagging process, completion criteria, and handover documentation requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define practical completion objectively: building substantially complete, fit for intended use, all regulatory approvals obtained, and snagging list agreed.

risk_patterns.name: AI Explainability Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system deployment without explainability requirements for automated decisions
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 22 and EU AI Act require explanation of automated decisions affecting individuals.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance; inability to respond to explanation requests; biased decision challenges.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for explainability requirements: decision rationale logging, human-readable explanations, bias monitoring, and appeal mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require decision rationale logging, human-readable explanations for affected individuals, quarterly bias audits, and appeal mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: No Penetration Testing Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud or SaaS contract without customer penetration testing rights or provider testing obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without pen testing rights, customer cannot independently verify the security posture of the service.
risk_patterns.impact: Unverified security posture; unknown vulnerabilities in critical systems.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for pen testing clause: customer testing rights, provider annual testing, results sharing, and remediation timelines.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual provider pen testing with results sharing, customer testing rights with coordination, and critical finding remediation within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare System Interoperability Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare IT contract without interoperability standards (HL7 FHIR) or integration testing
risk_patterns.description: Healthcare systems without interoperability standards create data silos and patient safety risks.
risk_patterns.impact: Data silos preventing clinical data sharing; patient safety risks from incomplete records.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For healthcare IT contracts, check for HL7 FHIR compliance, integration testing, data exchange standards, and interoperability certification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require HL7 FHIR R4 compliance, pre-deployment integration testing, IHE profile compliance, and interoperability testing with existing systems.

risk_patterns.name: M&A Key Employee Retention Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A agreement without key employee retention mechanisms post-completion
risk_patterns.description: Key employees often leave after acquisition, taking critical knowledge and customer relationships.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of key talent post-acquisition destroying 20-40% of deal value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for key employee identification, retention packages, non-compete agreements, and garden leave provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include pre-signing retention packages for top 10-20 employees, 12-month lock-in with good leaver provisions, and knowledge transfer obligations.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Studies show 47% of key employees leave within 12 months of acquisition.

risk_patterns.name: No Vulnerability Disclosure Process
risk_patterns.trigger: Software supply contract without coordinated vulnerability disclosure process
risk_patterns.description: Without coordinated disclosure, vulnerabilities may be publicly disclosed before patches are available.
risk_patterns.impact: Exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities; reputational damage from public disclosure without fix.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for vulnerability disclosure process: reporting channel, response timeline, coordinated disclosure, and patch delivery SLA.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include coordinated vulnerability disclosure: dedicated reporting channel, 24-hour acknowledgment, 90-day disclosure window, and patch delivery SLA.

risk_patterns.name: Government Social Value Delivery Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Government contract with social value commitments without delivery mechanism or measurement
risk_patterns.description: Social value commitments scored in procurement become contractual obligations that must be delivered.
risk_patterns.impact: Contract breach from failure to deliver social value commitments; reputational damage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if social value commitments from bid are incorporated, with delivery milestones, measurement methodology, and reporting requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Incorporate social value as schedule with SMART targets, quarterly reporting, TOMs-based measurement, and remediation plan for underdelivery.

risk_patterns.name: No Supply Chain Visibility Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical supply contract without sub-tier supplier visibility or concentration risk assessment
risk_patterns.description: Without supply chain visibility, concentration risks and single points of failure remain hidden.
risk_patterns.impact: Supply chain disruption from unknown sub-tier supplier failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for supply chain transparency: Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier disclosure, concentration risk assessment, and alternative sourcing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require supply chain mapping to Tier 2, concentration risk disclosure, alternative sourcing plans, and annual supply chain risk assessment.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: COVID-19 and Suez Canal blockage exposed hidden single-source dependencies in global supply chains.

risk_patterns.name: Remote Work Jurisdiction Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract permitting remote work without jurisdiction limitations
risk_patterns.description: Employees working from other jurisdictions may trigger permanent establishment, tax, and employment law obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Unplanned tax obligations, PE risk, and foreign employment law compliance requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for remote work jurisdiction limits, maximum days abroad, PE risk assessment, and compliance monitoring.
risk_patterns.remediation: Limit remote work to specified jurisdictions, cap foreign working days at 30 per year, require advance approval, and include PE risk assessment.

risk_patterns.name: Financial Services ICT Exit Strategy Missing
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services ICT contract without DORA-compliant exit strategy
risk_patterns.description: DORA Article 28 requires documented exit strategies for all critical ICT service arrangements.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance; inability to demonstrate ICT exit capability to supervisors.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for DORA exit strategy: documented plan, transition testing, data portability, knowledge transfer, and regular review.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include exit strategy as contract schedule: transition plan, annual testing, data export in standard formats, and 12-month transition assistance.

risk_patterns.name: No Incident Response Coordination
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without defined incident response coordination between parties
risk_patterns.description: Without coordinated incident response, security incidents are handled inefficiently with delayed containment.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended incident duration from uncoordinated response; increased breach impact.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident response: joint response plan, communication channels, escalation contacts, and post-incident review.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include joint incident response plan: 24/7 security contacts, 1-hour initial notification, coordinated containment, and post-incident review within 30 days.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Construction Defects Liability Period
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract with defects liability period less than 12 months
risk_patterns.description: Short defects liability periods expire before seasonal defects (e.g., heating, drainage) can manifest.
risk_patterns.impact: Employer bears remediation costs for defects discovered after short DLP.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check defects liability period: minimum 12 months from practical completion, with extension for remedied defects.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set DLP at minimum 12 months (24 months for complex works), with DLP extension for items requiring remediation, and latent defects period of 6-12 years.

risk_patterns.name: No AI Bias Monitoring Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: AI decision system contract without bias monitoring or fairness testing requirements
risk_patterns.description: AI systems can develop bias over time as data distributions shift, creating discrimination risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Discrimination claims, regulatory enforcement, and reputational damage from biased AI decisions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for bias monitoring: protected characteristic testing, demographic parity metrics, regular audits, and remediation process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require quarterly bias audits across protected characteristics, defined fairness metrics, drift monitoring, and remediation within 30 days of detected bias.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Amazon scrapped AI recruiting tool after discovering gender bias; multiple credit scoring algorithms found to be racially biased.

risk_patterns.name: No Configuration Management Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Managed service contract without configuration management and change tracking
risk_patterns.description: Without configuration management, unauthorised changes create instability and make incident diagnosis difficult.
risk_patterns.impact: Service instability from untracked changes; extended incident resolution times.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for configuration management: CMDB, change tracking, approval process, and rollback capability.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require CMDB maintenance, all changes logged and approved, rollback capability for all changes, and monthly configuration audit.

risk_patterns.name: No Scalability Provisions for Growth
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without provisions for scaling up or down based on business needs
risk_patterns.description: Rigid service scoping without scalability provisions creates bottlenecks during growth or waste during contraction.
risk_patterns.impact: Service constraints during growth; paying for unused capacity during contraction.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for scalability provisions: upscale lead times, downscale rights, minimum commitment flexibility, and scaling SLAs.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include scaling provisions: 30-day notice for 20% increase, quarterly downscale rights, +/-20% commitment flexibility, and scaling performance SLAs.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare Software Medical Device Classification Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare software contract without MDR classification assessment
risk_patterns.description: Software qualifying as a medical device under EU MDR requires conformity assessment, CE marking, and post-market surveillance.
risk_patterns.impact: Product withdrawal from market; regulatory prosecution for unclassified medical devices.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For healthcare software, check for MDR classification assessment, CE marking status, notified body involvement, and post-market surveillance plan.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require MDR classification assessment, CE marking for applicable classes, notified body approval, and ongoing post-market surveillance obligation.

risk_patterns.name: Construction CDM Regulations Compliance Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without clear CDM duty holder appointments and responsibilities
risk_patterns.description: CDM Regulations require appointment of principal designer and principal contractor with specific duties.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution and HSE enforcement for CDM non-compliance; project stop orders.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for CDM appointments: principal designer, principal contractor, and clear allocation of CDM duties between parties.
risk_patterns.remediation: Explicitly appoint CDM duty holders, allocate responsibilities, require pre-construction information, and mandate construction phase H&S plan.

risk_patterns.name: No SaaS Disaster Recovery Guarantees
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS contract without defined RPO/RTO, backup requirements, or DR testing
risk_patterns.description: SaaS providers may lack adequate DR capability; without contractual requirements, customer data is at risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete data loss or extended outage from SaaS provider infrastructure failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for RPO/RTO commitments, backup frequency, geographic redundancy, DR testing frequency, and customer notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require RPO <1 hour, RTO <4 hours, daily backups with 30-day retention, geographic redundancy, and annual DR testing with results sharing.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: OVHcloud data centre fire in 2021 destroyed customer data for providers without geographic redundancy.

risk_patterns.name: AI Act High-Risk System Without Conformity Assessment
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system classified as high-risk under EU AI Act without conformity assessment provisions
risk_patterns.description: High-risk AI systems require conformity assessment, CE marking, and ongoing monitoring before deployment.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines up to EUR 35M or 7% turnover; prohibition on system deployment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if AI system falls under Annex III high-risk categories. Verify conformity assessment, CE marking, and quality management provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AI Act compliance schedule: risk classification, conformity assessment, CE marking, quality management system, and post-market monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: AI Act Transparency Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system deployment without transparency obligations for users and affected persons
risk_patterns.description: EU AI Act requires transparency about AI interaction, emotion recognition, and deepfake generation.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory fines up to EUR 15M or 3% turnover for transparency violations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for transparency obligations: AI interaction disclosure, emotion recognition notification, and content marking for generative AI.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include transparency requirements: clear AI interaction notification, emotion recognition opt-in, content labelling for AI-generated material, and user documentation.

risk_patterns.name: NIS2 Management Body Liability Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: NIS2-covered contract without provisions addressing management body cybersecurity liability
risk_patterns.description: NIS2 imposes personal liability on management bodies for cybersecurity measures; contracts must support this.
risk_patterns.impact: Personal liability of directors for inadequate cybersecurity; management body sanctions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For NIS2 essential or important entities, check for management oversight provisions, training, and accountability mechanisms.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include NIS2 management provisions: cybersecurity training, oversight reporting, risk assessment involvement, and incident response accountability.

risk_patterns.name: Cyber Resilience Act Vulnerability Reporting Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Software product contract without CRA-mandated vulnerability reporting to ENISA
risk_patterns.description: EU CRA requires manufacturers to report actively exploited vulnerabilities to ENISA within 24 hours.
risk_patterns.impact: CRA fines and product withdrawal for failure to report vulnerabilities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For software product contracts, check for vulnerability reporting obligations: 24-hour ENISA notification, 72-hour detailed report, and customer notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include CRA vulnerability reporting: 24-hour ENISA notification, 72-hour detailed report, customer notification, and coordinated disclosure.

risk_patterns.name: Undocumented Joint Controller Relationship
risk_patterns.trigger: Data sharing arrangement without assessing whether a joint controller relationship exists
risk_patterns.description: Joint controller relationships require Article 26 arrangements; failure to document creates regulatory risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Joint and several liability for GDPR violations; regulatory penalties for undocumented arrangements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Assess whether both parties determine purposes and means of processing. If so, check for Article 26 joint controller agreement.
risk_patterns.remediation: Document joint controller arrangement per Article 26: define respective responsibilities, data subject contact point, and transparency information.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: CJEU Fashion ID case established that joint controllership arises more broadly than many expected.

risk_patterns.name: No Legitimate Interest Assessment Documented
risk_patterns.trigger: Data processing based on legitimate interest without documented balancing test
risk_patterns.description: Legitimate interest requires documented balancing test; without it, the processing basis may be challenged.
risk_patterns.impact: Processing basis invalidated; retrospective unlawfulness of processing.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For processing based on Article 6(1)(f), check for documented legitimate interest assessment including necessity and balancing test.
risk_patterns.remediation: Document LIA covering: legitimate interest identified, necessity of processing, balancing against data subject rights, and safeguards applied.

risk_patterns.name: CSRD Supply Chain Reporting Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply contract with CSRD-reporting entity without data provision obligations
risk_patterns.description: CSRD requires value chain data from suppliers; without contractual data obligations, reporting is incomplete.
risk_patterns.impact: Qualified CSRD disclosure; investor and regulatory scrutiny for incomplete reporting.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if customer is CSRD-subject. If so, verify supply contract includes ESG data provision aligned to ESRS requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ESRS-aligned data provision: environmental (E1-E5), social (S1-S4), and governance (G1) metrics, annual reporting, and audit cooperation.

risk_patterns.name: DORA ICT Concentration Risk Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services ICT contract without concentration risk assessment and mitigation
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires financial entities to assess and manage ICT third-party concentration risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory intervention requiring contract changes or provider diversification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for concentration risk provisions: multi-provider strategy, substitutability assessment, and regulatory review cooperation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include concentration risk provisions: annual assessment, substitutability plan, multi-cloud capability, and cooperation with ESA oversight.

risk_patterns.name: Children's Data Processing Without Age Verification
risk_patterns.trigger: Service potentially used by children without age verification or parental consent mechanisms
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 8 and ICO Children's Code require specific protections for children's data processing.
risk_patterns.impact: Enhanced GDPR penalties; ICO enforcement; reputational damage from children's data mishandling.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Assess if service is likely to be accessed by children. Check for age verification, parental consent mechanism, and children-specific privacy notice.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include age verification mechanism, parental consent for under-16s, children-specific privacy notice, and data minimisation for children's data.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: ICO has fined platforms for inadequate children's data protection under the Age-Appropriate Design Code.

risk_patterns.name: Worker Classification Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Contractor engagement without IR35/worker classification assessment
risk_patterns.description: Misclassification of workers as contractors creates tax liability, employment rights claims, and HMRC penalties.
risk_patterns.impact: Retrospective PAYE liability, NICs, and penalties; employment rights claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contractor engagements, check for IR35 status determination, right of substitution, mutuality of obligation, and control factors.
risk_patterns.remediation: Conduct IR35 assessment before engagement, document status determination statement, include substitution rights, and limit control factors.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: HMRC has collected hundreds of millions in IR35 settlements from major organisations.

risk_patterns.name: GDPR Automated Decision-Making Without Safeguards
risk_patterns.trigger: Automated decision-making producing legal effects without Article 22 safeguards
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 22 restricts solely automated decision-making with legal or significant effects on individuals.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR fines; individual compensation claims; injunctive relief against processing.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if processing involves solely automated decisions with legal/significant effects. Verify safeguards: human intervention, right to contest, and explanation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include Article 22 safeguards: human review of significant decisions, right to contest, right to explanation, and regular bias review.

risk_patterns.name: No Transfer Impact Assessment for International Transfers
risk_patterns.trigger: International data transfer relying on SCCs without transfer impact assessment
risk_patterns.description: Post-Schrems II, transfer impact assessments are required to verify adequate protection in recipient country.
risk_patterns.impact: Unlawful data transfers; GDPR enforcement; data transfer injunctions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if TIA has been conducted for each transfer destination, assessing surveillance laws and supplementary measures needed.
risk_patterns.remediation: Conduct TIA for each destination country, document findings, implement supplementary measures (encryption, pseudonymisation), and review annually.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple DPAs have ordered transfers suspended where TIAs were inadequate.

risk_patterns.name: Working Time Directive Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment or contractor engagement without working time regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: Working Time Regulations limit hours, require rest breaks, and mandate minimum annual leave.
risk_patterns.impact: Employment tribunal claims, HSE enforcement, and insurance invalidation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for working time compliance: 48-hour weekly limit, rest periods, annual leave entitlement, and record keeping.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include WTR compliance: 48-hour opt-out must be voluntary, ensure rest periods, guarantee minimum 5.6 weeks annual leave, and maintain records.

risk_patterns.name: PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Payment services contract without PSD2 SCA compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: PSD2 requires strong customer authentication for electronic payments; non-compliance creates liability shifts.
risk_patterns.impact: Fraud liability shifts to non-compliant party; regulatory sanctions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For payment service contracts, check for SCA compliance, exemption strategy, and liability allocation for SCA failures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require PSD2 SCA compliance, document exemption strategies (TRA, white-listing), and allocate fraud liability for SCA failures.

risk_patterns.name: EMIR Derivatives Reporting Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Derivatives contract without EMIR reporting and clearing obligation provisions
risk_patterns.description: EMIR requires trade reporting and central clearing for standardised OTC derivatives.
risk_patterns.impact: EMIR regulatory penalties and potential trade voiding.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For derivatives contracts, check for EMIR reporting obligations, clearing thresholds, margin requirements, and portfolio reconciliation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include EMIR compliance clause: dual-sided reporting, clearing obligation assessment, margin exchange requirements, and portfolio reconciliation.

risk_patterns.name: MiFID II Best Execution Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Investment services agreement without MiFID II best execution policy and reporting
risk_patterns.description: MiFID II requires best execution policy, monitoring, and annual reporting for investment firms.
risk_patterns.impact: FCA enforcement; customer compensation for execution failures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For investment services agreements, check for best execution policy, execution venue disclosure, and annual quality reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include best execution clause: execution policy disclosure, venue assessment, annual RTS 28 reporting, and customer complaint mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: Solvency II Outsourcing Compliance Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Insurance company outsourcing contract without Solvency II outsourcing provisions
risk_patterns.description: Solvency II requires specific provisions for outsourcing of critical functions by insurance undertakings.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance; supervisory intervention in outsourcing arrangement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For insurance company outsourcing, check for Solvency II provisions: supervisory access, audit rights, BCP requirements, and performance monitoring.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include Solvency II outsourcing provisions: supervisory access, unrestricted audit rights, BCP/DR, performance monitoring, and governance reporting.

risk_patterns.name: GDPR 72-Hour Breach Notification Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA with processor breach notification timeframe exceeding 72 hours total
risk_patterns.description: Controllers must notify DPAs within 72 hours of becoming aware; processor notification must leave adequate time.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR breach notification violations from insufficient time to assess and report.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check processor breach notification timeframe: should be maximum 24-36 hours to leave controller time for assessment and DPA notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Set processor notification at 24 hours maximum, include preliminary notification format, and require assistance with DPA notification and DPIA.
