risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple organisations fined for late breach notification where processor delays consumed the 72-hour window.

risk_patterns.name: EU Pay Transparency Directive Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment or staffing contract without pay transparency measures for June 2026 deadline
risk_patterns.description: EU Pay Transparency Directive requires pay range disclosure, pay gap reporting, and equal pay assessments.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory penalties and equal pay claims from non-compliance with transparency requirements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for pay transparency provisions: pre-employment pay range disclosure, pay gap reporting obligations, and equal pay assessment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include pay transparency clause: pay range in job postings, annual gender pay gap reporting for 100+ employees, and corrective measures for gaps >5%.

risk_patterns.name: Building Safety Act Compliance Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Higher-risk building construction contract without Building Safety Act compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: Building Safety Act 2022 creates new duty holder roles and golden thread information requirements for higher-risk buildings.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal penalties, project stop notices, and personal liability for duty holders.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For buildings >18m/7 storeys, check for BSA compliance: accountable person, building safety manager, golden thread, and gateway approvals.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include BSA compliance: duty holder appointments, golden thread information management, gateway approval cooperation, and ongoing safety case.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Post-Grenfell regulations creating new criminal offences for building safety failures.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Localisation Compliance for Regulated Data
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract processing regulated data without data localisation requirements for specific jurisdictions
risk_patterns.description: Certain jurisdictions and sectors mandate data localisation for specific categories of regulated data.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance; data access orders; operational disruption from forced data migration.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if data categories have localisation requirements: financial data (DORA), health data (national laws), government data (sovereignty), telecom data.
risk_patterns.remediation: Map data categories to localisation requirements, implement storage location restrictions, and include contractual data residency guarantees.

risk_patterns.name: GDPR Right to Erasure Implementation Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Data processing contract without mechanism to support erasure requests across all systems
risk_patterns.description: Right to erasure requires deletion across all systems, backups, and sub-processors within 30 days.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR violations from incomplete erasure; DPA complaints and enforcement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for erasure mechanism: request process, system coverage, backup handling, sub-processor notification, and confirmation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include erasure mechanism: 30-day response, deletion across all live systems, backup deletion at next rotation cycle, sub-processor notification, and confirmation.

risk_patterns.name: Employment Equality Act Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment or staffing contract without anti-discrimination and equal opportunities provisions
risk_patterns.description: Equality Act 2010 prohibits discrimination; employers can be vicariously liable for contractor discrimination.
risk_patterns.impact: Employment tribunal claims, uncapped compensation, and reputational damage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for equality provisions: anti-discrimination commitment, reasonable adjustments, training obligations, and complaint mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include equality clause: anti-discrimination commitment, reasonable adjustments obligation, equality training, and protected characteristic monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: Digital Content Conformity Requirement
risk_patterns.trigger: B2C digital content or service contract without statutory conformity requirements
risk_patterns.description: Consumer Rights Act 2015 (UK) and Digital Content Directive (EU) impose minimum conformity standards for digital content.
risk_patterns.impact: Mandatory repair/replace remedies; price reduction or refund rights that cannot be excluded.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For B2C digital content, check for conformity requirements: satisfactory quality, fitness for purpose, as described, and security updates.
risk_patterns.remediation: Ensure contract does not exclude statutory conformity rights. Include security update obligations and conformity monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: AIFMD Fund Management Delegation Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Fund management delegation contract without AIFMD delegation requirements
risk_patterns.description: AIFMD restricts delegation of portfolio management and risk management with specific regulatory requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory sanctions; fund licence risk from non-compliant delegation arrangements.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For fund management delegation, check for AIFMD provisions: delegation rationale, oversight, recall rights, and regulatory notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AIFMD delegation provisions: documented rationale, ongoing oversight, recall capability, regulatory notification, and letterbox entity prevention.

risk_patterns.name: New EU Product Liability Directive Exposure
risk_patterns.trigger: Product supply or software contract not addressing revised EU Product Liability Directive provisions
risk_patterns.description: Revised PLD extends product liability to software, AI systems, and digital manufacturing files.
risk_patterns.impact: Strict product liability for software defects; reversed burden of proof for complex products.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if software or digital products are covered by revised PLD. Verify liability allocation, insurance coverage, and defect reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Address revised PLD: classify software as product, allocate liability between components, require product liability insurance, and implement defect monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: EU Electronic Communications Code Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Telecommunications service contract without EECC compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: EECC imposes security obligations, transparency requirements, and switching rights for telecom services.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory fines and contract terms deemed void for EECC non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For telecom service contracts, check for EECC compliance: security measures, contract summary, switching rights, and service quality standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include EECC compliance provisions: security incident reporting, contract summary sheet, number portability, and maximum 24-month term for consumers.

risk_patterns.name: Clinical Trial Regulation Non-Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Clinical trial agreement without EU Clinical Trial Regulation compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: EU CTR requires specific provisions for sponsor-site agreements, informed consent, and safety reporting.
risk_patterns.impact: Trial suspension; regulatory sanctions; participant safety risk.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For clinical trial contracts, check for CTR compliance: sponsor responsibilities, investigator obligations, safety reporting, and CTIS registration.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include CTR compliance: sponsor oversight obligations, investigator qualification, SAE reporting within 24 hours, CTIS registration, and insurance coverage.

risk_patterns.name: Energy Sector Regulatory Compliance Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Energy sector contract without specific energy regulatory compliance provisions
risk_patterns.description: Energy sector has extensive licensing, reporting, and market conduct regulations that must be reflected in contracts.
risk_patterns.impact: Licence condition breach; regulatory enforcement; market access restrictions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For energy sector contracts, check for licence condition compliance, regulatory reporting, market conduct rules, and REMIT obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include energy regulatory clause: licence condition compliance, REMIT reporting, emissions trading compliance, and regulatory change mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: AI Output Ownership Ambiguity
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without clear ownership allocation for AI-generated outputs
risk_patterns.description: AI-generated content may not qualify for copyright protection; ownership must be explicitly allocated.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed ownership of AI-generated deliverables; inability to protect or licence outputs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for AI output ownership clause: who owns generated content, licence to training improvements, and derivative works rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate AI output ownership to customer, with provider retaining aggregated/anonymised learning. Include warranty that outputs do not infringe third-party rights.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: US Copyright Office has ruled AI-generated works without human authorship are not copyrightable.

risk_patterns.name: Prompt Engineering IP Unaddressed
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract without allocation of IP rights in custom prompts and prompt libraries
risk_patterns.description: Custom prompts represent significant investment; without ownership allocation, valuable IP is unprotected.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of competitive advantage from custom prompts; provider reuse of customer prompt IP.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for prompt IP allocation: customer-developed prompts, provider prompt libraries, and hybrid prompt ownership.
risk_patterns.remediation: Customer owns all custom-developed prompts and prompt libraries. Provider retains base prompt templates. Non-exclusive licence for provider to use anonymised patterns.

risk_patterns.name: Synthetic Data Ownership Unallocated
risk_patterns.trigger: AI contract generating synthetic data without ownership and usage rights allocation
risk_patterns.description: Synthetic data generated from customer data may contain derivative works; ownership must be explicitly allocated.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed ownership of synthetic training data; potential data protection issues.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for synthetic data ownership: generation rights, usage restrictions, sharing limitations, and data protection compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate synthetic data ownership to commissioning party, restrict provider reuse, and ensure GDPR compliance for data derived from personal data.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Design IP Ownership Dispute Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Design and build contract without clear allocation of design IP between architect, contractor, and employer
risk_patterns.description: Construction involves multiple design contributors; without clear IP allocation, usage and modification rights are disputed.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to modify building or reuse designs without designer consent.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for design IP allocation: architect's licence, contractor design IP, BIM model ownership, and employer modification rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Secure irrevocable licence to use and modify all designs for the project, BIM model ownership transfer, and third-party licence for future modifications.

risk_patterns.name: M&A IP Valuation and Chain of Title Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction involving significant IP assets without IP chain of title verification
risk_patterns.description: IP assets may have defective chain of title, undisclosed encumbrances, or inaccurate valuation.
risk_patterns.impact: Overpayment for IP assets or discovery of title defects destroying deal value.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check IP warranties: ownership, non-infringement, no encumbrances, licence validity, and employee/contractor assignment completeness.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require comprehensive IP due diligence, chain of title verification, freedom to operate opinions, and specific IP warranties with survival period.

risk_patterns.name: No IP Register Maintenance Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: IP-intensive contract without obligation to maintain IP registrations
risk_patterns.description: IP registrations (patents, trademarks) lapse without renewal; contractual obligation ensures protection continuity.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of registered IP rights from non-renewal or maintenance failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for IP maintenance obligations: registration renewal, jurisdiction filings, and notification of challenges or lapses.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include IP maintenance clause: timely renewal of registrations, notification of challenges, and indemnity for negligent lapses.

risk_patterns.name: No Technology Transfer Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: International technology contract without technology transfer regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: Many jurisdictions require approval for technology transfers; non-compliance creates criminal liability.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution, technology seizure, and contract voiding for unlicensed transfers.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For cross-border technology contracts, check for transfer approvals, dual-use classification, and end-use certificates.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include technology transfer clause: regulatory approval responsibility, dual-use classification, end-use restrictions, and re-transfer prohibitions.

risk_patterns.name: No IoT Data Ownership Allocation
risk_patterns.trigger: IoT or connected device contract without allocation of sensor and usage data ownership
risk_patterns.description: IoT devices generate valuable data; without ownership allocation, device manufacturers may claim data rights.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of operational data rights to device manufacturer; inability to switch providers.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For IoT contracts, check for data ownership: raw sensor data, derived insights, aggregated data, and API access rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate raw data and derived insights to customer, grant provider anonymised aggregation rights, and require API access for data extraction.

risk_patterns.name: No Domain Name and Brand Asset Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: Marketing or agency contract without protection of domain names and brand assets
risk_patterns.description: Agencies registering domains and brand assets in their own name create dependency and loss of control.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of control over domain names and brand assets on agency termination.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for brand asset ownership: domain registration in client name, social media account ownership, and brand asset return on termination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require all domains registered in client name, social media accounts owned by client, and complete brand asset handover within 30 days of termination.

risk_patterns.name: No Software Licence Compliance Audit Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Enterprise software licence without compliance audit provisions
risk_patterns.description: Software licence non-compliance creates infringement liability; audit provisions enable both parties to verify compliance.
risk_patterns.impact: Unexpected true-up costs or infringement claims from licence non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for licence audit clause: scope, frequency, notice, cooperation obligations, and consequences of non-compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include balanced audit clause: annual right with 30-day notice, scope limited to licence compliance, 90-day cure period, and cost allocation.

risk_patterns.name: Employee Invention Rights Not Addressed
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract without clear employee invention assignment and compensation provisions
risk_patterns.description: Employee inventions may belong to the employee under patent law unless properly assigned; compensation may be required.
risk_patterns.impact: Employee claims to inventions created during employment; compensation claims for outstanding benefit.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check employment contract for invention assignment, duty to disclose, compensation provisions, and moral rights waiver.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include invention assignment clause: assignment of employment inventions, duty to disclose, reasonable compensation for outstanding benefit, and moral rights waiver.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: UK Patents Act s.40 allows employees to claim compensation for inventions of outstanding benefit to the employer.

risk_patterns.name: Database Rights Not Allocated
risk_patterns.trigger: Data processing or analytics contract without allocation of sui generis database rights
risk_patterns.description: EU database right protects substantial investment in data compilation; without allocation, rights may default to the compiler.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of database rights to data processor or analytics provider.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For data processing contracts, check for database rights allocation distinct from copyright and data ownership.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate database rights to commissioning party, grant processor limited extraction rights for service delivery, and require return on termination.

risk_patterns.name: Inadequate Trade Secret Protection Measures
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving trade secrets without adequate protection measures per EU Trade Secrets Directive
risk_patterns.description: EU Trade Secrets Directive requires reasonable steps to maintain secrecy; contractual measures are a key element.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of trade secret protection status due to inadequate contractual measures.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for trade secret protection: identification, access controls, need-to-know restrictions, employee obligations, and return/destruction provisions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include trade secret schedule: identify trade secrets, restrict access to named individuals, require NDAs, implement technical access controls, and audit rights.

risk_patterns.name: Unclear Content Licence Scope
risk_patterns.trigger: Content creation or media contract with vague licence scope for created works
risk_patterns.description: Vague licence scope leads to disputes over usage rights, platforms, territories, and duration.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to use content as intended; infringement claims from exceeding implied licence.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check content licence for: scope (exclusive/non-exclusive), territory, duration, platforms, modification rights, and sublicensing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define content licence precisely: exclusive/non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual or term, all media and platforms, right to modify, and sublicensing rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Open Source Policy in Software Development
risk_patterns.trigger: Software development contract without open source usage policy or approval process
risk_patterns.description: Unrestricted open source usage can introduce copyleft contamination, security vulnerabilities, and licence conflicts.
risk_patterns.impact: Copyleft contamination of proprietary code; mandatory source code disclosure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for open source policy: approved licences, prohibited licences (GPL for proprietary), approval process, and SBOM delivery.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include open source policy: whitelist permissive licences (MIT, Apache 2.0), prohibit strong copyleft in proprietary components, require approval for new dependencies.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Breach Liability Allocation
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract involving personal data without clear liability allocation for data breaches
risk_patterns.description: Without breach liability allocation, both parties face disputed costs and regulatory fines.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed breach costs including notification, credit monitoring, forensics, and regulatory fines.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for breach liability allocation: fault-based allocation, cost categories, indemnification, insurance requirements, and caps.
risk_patterns.remediation: Allocate breach liability based on fault: responsible party bears notification costs, credit monitoring, forensics, and regulatory fines. Include breach response insurance.

risk_patterns.name: No DPIA Cooperation Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without obligation for processor to assist with data protection impact assessments
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 35 requires DPIAs for high-risk processing; processor cooperation is essential but often not contractualised.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to complete mandatory DPIAs; delayed processing activities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for DPIA cooperation clause: information provision, risk assessment input, and technical measure documentation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include DPIA cooperation: processor to provide processing details, security measure documentation, and risk assessment input within 30 days of request.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Sovereignty Guarantees
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud contract without data sovereignty guarantees against foreign government access
risk_patterns.description: US CLOUD Act and FISA 702 can compel US providers to disclose data regardless of storage location.
risk_patterns.impact: Compelled disclosure to foreign governments; GDPR violation; loss of client trust.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data sovereignty: provider jurisdiction, foreign government access policy, notification obligations, and legal challenge commitment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require EU-sovereign operations, commitment to challenge foreign government orders, customer notification, and technical measures (encryption with customer-held keys).
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Schrems II raised fundamental questions about US provider data sovereignty; EU Commission seeking EU-US Data Privacy Framework compliance.

risk_patterns.name: AI Training on Personal Data Without Lawful Basis
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract allowing model training on customer data without explicit consent or lawful basis
risk_patterns.description: Using personal data for AI model training requires specific lawful basis and purpose limitation compliance.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR violations from unlawful processing for AI training; DPA enforcement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if AI provider can use customer data for model training. Verify lawful basis, purpose limitation, and opt-out mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Prohibit AI training on customer personal data by default, require explicit opt-in with documented lawful basis, and ensure model isolation between customers.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Italian DPA temporarily banned ChatGPT for GDPR violations related to training data processing.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Processing Records Provision
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without obligation for processor to maintain and share processing activity records
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 30 requires processors to maintain records; controllers need access for their own Article 30 compliance.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR Article 30 non-compliance; inability to demonstrate processing accountability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for processing records obligation: record format, update frequency, sharing mechanism, and categories covered.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require processor to maintain Article 30(2) records, share annually, and update within 14 days of processing changes.

risk_patterns.name: No Standard Contractual Clauses for Transfers
risk_patterns.trigger: International data transfer without execution of appropriate SCC module
risk_patterns.description: Post-Schrems II, SCCs are the primary transfer mechanism for most international data transfers.
risk_patterns.impact: Unlawful data transfers; GDPR fines; supervisory authority transfer prohibition orders.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if data transfers outside EEA. Verify appropriate SCC module executed: Module 1 (C2C), Module 2 (C2P), Module 3 (P2P), or Module 4 (P2C).
risk_patterns.remediation: Execute appropriate SCC module with completed annexes, conduct TIA for recipient country, and implement supplementary measures where needed.

risk_patterns.name: No Cookie and Tracking Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Website or app development contract without cookie consent and tracking compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: ePrivacy Directive requires informed consent for non-essential cookies; widespread enforcement by DPAs.
risk_patterns.impact: DPA fines for non-compliant cookie practices; class action risk under GDPR.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For website/app contracts, check for cookie compliance: consent mechanism, cookie audit, retention periods, and opt-out functionality.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require GDPR-compliant cookie consent: prior consent for non-essential cookies, granular choices, easy withdrawal, and monthly cookie audit.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple DPAs have fined organisations for cookie consent violations (CNIL fined Google EUR 150M).

risk_patterns.name: No Vendor Security Assessment Before Processing
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without pre-engagement security assessment of processor technical and organisational measures
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 28(1) requires controllers to use processors providing sufficient guarantees; this must be verified.
risk_patterns.impact: Controller liability for choosing processor without adequate security; breach incident costs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for pre-engagement security assessment: questionnaire, certification review, pen test results, and ongoing monitoring.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require pre-engagement security assessment, ISO 27001 or SOC 2 certification, annual pen test results, and ongoing security monitoring rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Loss Prevention Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract handling sensitive data without DLP technology and monitoring requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without DLP controls, sensitive data can be exfiltrated through email, USB, cloud storage, or printing.
risk_patterns.impact: Undetected data exfiltration; large-scale data breaches from insider threats.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for DLP requirements: email scanning, endpoint controls, cloud access security, print monitoring, and USB restrictions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require DLP deployment: email content scanning, endpoint controls for USB/print, CASB for cloud, and automated alerting for policy violations.

risk_patterns.name: No Privacy Notice Alignment Check
risk_patterns.trigger: Processing activities not reflected in customer-facing privacy notices
risk_patterns.description: GDPR transparency requires processing activities to be accurately reflected in privacy notices.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR transparency violations; data subject complaints; DPA investigations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if all processing activities described in DPA are covered by the controller's privacy notice.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require processor to notify controller of new processing activities 30 days in advance to allow privacy notice updates.

risk_patterns.name: No Clear Security Incident Definition
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA with vague definition of what constitutes a notifiable security incident or personal data breach
risk_patterns.description: Without clear definitions, processor may not recognise or report incidents that constitute personal data breaches.
risk_patterns.impact: Under-reporting of security incidents; delayed breach notification to DPA.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clear security incident definition: categories of incidents, personal data breach definition, and near-miss reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define security incident broadly: unauthorised access, loss, alteration, or disclosure of personal data. Include near-miss reporting for risk trend analysis.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Protection Training Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without data protection training requirements for processor personnel
risk_patterns.description: Untrained staff are the primary cause of data breaches; training requirements are a basic GDPR accountability measure.
risk_patterns.impact: Increased breach likelihood from untrained personnel; accountability demonstration failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for training requirements: initial training, annual refresher, role-specific training, and training record maintenance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require annual data protection training for all personnel processing personal data, role-specific training for high-risk processing, and training record maintenance.

risk_patterns.name: No Pseudonymisation Requirements for Analytics
risk_patterns.trigger: Analytics or research processing without pseudonymisation or anonymisation requirements
risk_patterns.description: GDPR encourages pseudonymisation as a safeguard; analytics without it creates unnecessary risk of re-identification.
risk_patterns.impact: Re-identification risk in analytics datasets; failure to apply data protection by design.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For analytics processing, check for pseudonymisation requirements, anonymisation standards, and re-identification risk assessment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require pseudonymisation for analytics: key separation, access controls on re-identification, and regular re-identification risk assessment.

risk_patterns.name: Cloud Backup Data Protection Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud service contract where backup data is stored in different jurisdiction or with different security controls
risk_patterns.description: Backup data often has weaker controls and may be stored in jurisdictions not covered by the main DPA.
risk_patterns.impact: Data protection violations from backup data stored outside agreed jurisdictions with weaker controls.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if backup locations are covered by DPA, same security controls apply to backups, and backup retention aligns with data retention policy.
risk_patterns.remediation: Ensure backup locations are within agreed jurisdictions, same encryption and access controls apply, and backup retention matches data retention policy.

risk_patterns.name: No Incident Forensics Cooperation Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: DPA without obligation for processor to cooperate with forensic investigation after breach
risk_patterns.description: Without forensic cooperation, controller cannot determine breach scope, affected individuals, or root cause.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to assess breach scope; delayed and incomplete DPA notification; regulatory criticism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for forensic cooperation: evidence preservation, investigation access, expert cooperation, and root cause analysis sharing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include forensic cooperation: preserve evidence, provide investigation access within 24 hours, cooperate with external forensics, and share root cause analysis.

risk_patterns.name: No ESG Representation in Supply Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply contract without ESG compliance representations from supplier
risk_patterns.description: Supply chain ESG failures create reputational damage for the contracting organisation.
risk_patterns.impact: Reputational damage from supply chain ESG violations; investor and consumer backlash.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for ESG representations: environmental compliance, labour standards, anti-corruption, and human rights due diligence.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ESG representations: environmental regulatory compliance, ILO labour standards, anti-corruption commitment, and human rights due diligence.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple fashion brands suffered major reputational damage from supply chain labour violations.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Breach Communication Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without agreed approach to public communications following a data breach
risk_patterns.description: Uncoordinated breach communications create confusion, blame-shifting, and amplified reputational damage.
risk_patterns.impact: Reputational damage amplified by uncoordinated or contradictory breach communications.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for breach communication protocol: joint statement approval, single spokesperson, messaging alignment, and media response coordination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include breach communication protocol: joint approval of public statements within 24 hours, single point of contact, aligned messaging, and no blame attribution.

risk_patterns.name: AI Output Reputational Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Customer-facing AI deployment without content filtering and brand safety controls
risk_patterns.description: AI systems can generate offensive, biased, or factually incorrect content that damages brand reputation.
risk_patterns.impact: Viral reputational damage from inappropriate AI-generated content; customer trust erosion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for AI content controls: output filtering, brand safety guidelines, human review triggers, and escalation for harmful content.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require content filtering, brand safety guidelines, human review for high-risk outputs, real-time monitoring, and kill switch for content generation.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Microsoft Tay chatbot and multiple AI customer service incidents demonstrate AI reputational risk.

risk_patterns.name: No Subcontractor Brand Standards
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract where subcontractors interact with customers without brand and quality standards
risk_patterns.description: Subcontractors representing the brand without standards create inconsistent customer experiences.
risk_patterns.impact: Brand dilution and customer dissatisfaction from inconsistent subcontractor service quality.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for subcontractor brand standards: training requirements, quality monitoring, customer interaction guidelines, and brand compliance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require subcontractor brand training, quality monitoring, customer interaction guidelines, and right to remove subcontractors for brand non-compliance.

risk_patterns.name: No Supplier Whistleblower Channel
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply chain contract without access to whistleblower reporting channel for supplier workers
risk_patterns.description: Without reporting channels, supply chain misconduct goes undetected until it becomes public.
risk_patterns.impact: Undetected supply chain violations; reputational damage when issues become public.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for supplier whistleblower provisions: reporting channel access, anonymity protection, and investigation commitment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Provide supplier workers access to whistleblower channel, guarantee anonymity, commit to investigation, and include non-retaliation provisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Ethical AI Use Policy
risk_patterns.trigger: AI deployment contract without ethical use policy and prohibited use cases
risk_patterns.description: AI deployed for unethical purposes creates severe reputational damage even if legally permissible.
risk_patterns.impact: Public backlash from perceived unethical AI use; employee and customer trust erosion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for ethical AI policy: prohibited use cases, fairness requirements, transparency commitments, and ethical review process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ethical AI policy: prohibited uses (surveillance, manipulation, discrimination), fairness testing, transparency commitment, and ethics board review.

risk_patterns.name: No Customer Reference Approval Process
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without mutual approval requirement for case studies, references, and marketing use
risk_patterns.description: Unapproved references can disclose confidential relationships or misrepresent the engagement.
risk_patterns.impact: Confidentiality breach through unapproved marketing; misrepresented engagement scope.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for marketing approval clause: prior written consent for references, case studies, and logo use.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require mutual written approval for references, case studies, logo use, and marketing materials. Include pre-approved press release template.

risk_patterns.name: No Diversity and Inclusion Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Large procurement contract without diversity and inclusion requirements for service delivery team
risk_patterns.description: Procurement without D&I requirements misses social value opportunities and may attract criticism.
risk_patterns.impact: Missed social value commitments; criticism from stakeholders for ignoring D&I.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For large contracts, check for D&I requirements: diverse staffing, SME subcontracting, social enterprise engagement, and reporting.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include D&I requirements: diverse staffing targets, SME subcontracting goals, annual diversity reporting, and progressive improvement.

risk_patterns.name: Greenwashing Risk in Contractual Claims
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract containing unsubstantiated environmental or sustainability claims
risk_patterns.description: EU Green Claims Directive will make unsubstantiated environmental claims in contracts actionable.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory fines, contract challenges, and reputational damage from greenwashing claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for environmental claims: substantiation with data, third-party verification, and compliance with Green Claims Directive standards.
risk_patterns.remediation: Substantiate all environmental claims with verified data, third-party certification, and lifecycle assessment. Remove unverifiable claims.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Incident Communication Protocol
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without agreed communication protocol for major service incidents
risk_patterns.description: Poor incident communication erodes customer trust more than the incident itself.
risk_patterns.impact: Customer trust erosion from poor incident communication; social media amplification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident communication: status page, proactive notification, update frequency, and post-incident communication.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include incident communication protocol: status page within 15 minutes, hourly updates, executive notification for P1, and post-incident report within 5 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Cloud Exit Strategy
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud service contract without documented exit strategy and data migration plan
risk_patterns.description: Without exit strategy, organisation is locked into cloud provider with no practical migration path.
risk_patterns.impact: Indefinite vendor lock-in; prohibitive migration costs estimated at 2-5x annual spend.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for cloud exit strategy: data export format, migration timeline, parallel running, and technical assistance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include cloud exit strategy: data export in standard formats, 12-month migration window, technical assistance at prevailing rates, and API access during transition.

risk_patterns.name: No Ransomware Resilience Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service contract without ransomware-specific resilience and recovery provisions
risk_patterns.description: Ransomware is the leading cause of extended service outages; specific resilience measures are essential.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended service outage from ransomware; data loss; ransom payment pressure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for ransomware resilience: immutable backups, air-gapped recovery, detection capability, and recovery testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require immutable backups, air-gapped recovery environment, EDR deployment, 4-hour recovery SLA for ransomware events, and annual recovery testing.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Colonial Pipeline, Travelex, and numerous organisations suffered extended outages from ransomware attacks.

risk_patterns.name: No Geographic Diversity for Cloud Services
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud service without geographic diversity for disaster recovery across regions
risk_patterns.description: Single-region cloud deployments are vulnerable to regional outages, natural disasters, and infrastructure failures.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended outage from regional cloud infrastructure failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for geographic diversity: multi-region deployment, cross-region failover, and recovery testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require multi-region deployment with automatic failover, cross-region data replication, and annual regional failover testing.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: AWS us-east-1 outages have caused widespread service disruptions for single-region customers.

risk_patterns.name: No Pandemic Business Continuity Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without pandemic-specific business continuity provisions
risk_patterns.description: COVID-19 demonstrated that standard BCP plans were inadequate for pandemic scenarios.
risk_patterns.impact: Service disruption from workforce unavailability during pandemic events.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for pandemic BCP: remote working capability, split-team operations, essential service classification, and communication plan.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include pandemic BCP: 100% remote working capability, split-team operations, essential service prioritisation, and weekly status reporting.

risk_patterns.name: No Alternative Sourcing Strategy
risk_patterns.trigger: Single-source supply contract without alternative sourcing or dual-sourcing provisions
risk_patterns.description: Single-source dependency creates critical vulnerability to supplier failure, geopolitical events, or capacity constraints.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete supply disruption from single-source supplier failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Assess supply chain for single-source dependencies. Check for alternative sourcing provisions, safety stock, and dual-qualification rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include dual-sourcing strategy: qualify alternative supplier, maintain 30-day safety stock, and include break clause for persistent supply failures.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Global chip shortage demonstrated catastrophic impact of single-source semiconductor dependencies.

risk_patterns.name: No Cryptographic Key Material Recovery
risk_patterns.trigger: Encryption-dependent service without key material escrow or recovery provisions
risk_patterns.description: Loss of encryption keys renders all encrypted data permanently inaccessible.
risk_patterns.impact: Permanent loss of all encrypted data if key material is lost or corrupted.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for key management: key escrow, backup procedures, recovery testing, and key rotation with backward compatibility.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require key escrow with independent custodian, regular backup verification, annual recovery testing, and key rotation with backward compatibility.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Contractor Insolvency Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without protections against contractor insolvency during works
risk_patterns.description: Contractor insolvency during construction leaves partially completed works, unpaid subcontractors, and completion risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Completion costs 30-50% above original contract value when replacing insolvent contractor.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for insolvency protection: performance bond, parent company guarantee, step-in rights, and vesting of materials.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require performance bond (10%), parent company guarantee, step-in rights, vesting certificates for off-site materials, and direct payment provisions.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Carillion collapse left billions in uncompleted contracts and thousands of unpaid subcontractors.

risk_patterns.name: No Succession Plan for Key Software Vendor
risk_patterns.trigger: Dependency on software vendor without business continuity plan for vendor cessation
risk_patterns.description: Software vendor acquisition, pivot, or cessation can terminate access to business-critical applications.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of business-critical software requiring emergency replacement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for vendor cessation provisions: source code escrow, API documentation, data portability, and transition assistance.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require source code escrow with release triggers (insolvency, material breach, cessation of support), API documentation, and transition assistance.

risk_patterns.name: Financial Services Third-Party Failure Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services contract without third-party failure contingency and DORA exit testing
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires financial entities to test third-party failure scenarios and maintain exit capability.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory non-compliance; inability to demonstrate third-party failure resilience.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for third-party failure testing: annual exit scenario testing, alternative provider identification, and recovery timeline.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual exit testing, identified alternative providers, 30-day recovery timeline, and documented exit procedures. Report results to board.

risk_patterns.name: No Cyber Insurance for Business Continuity
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract for critical services without cyber insurance covering business interruption
risk_patterns.description: Cyber incidents causing extended outages create business interruption losses; cyber insurance provides financial resilience.
risk_patterns.impact: Uninsured business interruption losses from cyber incidents.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for cyber insurance: BI coverage, waiting period, coverage period, sub-limits, and incident response costs.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require cyber insurance with business interruption coverage: 8-hour waiting period, 120-day coverage, incident response sub-limit, and annual policy review.

risk_patterns.name: No Multi-Cloud or Cloud-Agnostic Strategy
risk_patterns.trigger: Cloud-dependent architecture locked to single provider without portability provisions
risk_patterns.description: Single-cloud dependency creates provider concentration risk and eliminates competitive leverage.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete infrastructure dependency on single provider; no competitive pricing leverage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for cloud portability: container-based deployment, cloud-agnostic services, data portability, and provider abstraction.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require container-based deployment, cloud-agnostic service selection where possible, documented migration procedures, and annual portability testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Regulatory-Driven Exit Capability
risk_patterns.trigger: Regulated entity contract without exit capability for regulatory direction to change provider
risk_patterns.description: Regulators may direct regulated entities to change providers; without exit capability, compliance is impossible.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to comply with regulatory direction; supervisory escalation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For regulated entity contracts, check for regulatory exit clause: immediate termination for regulatory direction, expedited transition, and cost allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include regulatory exit clause: immediate termination right for regulatory direction, 90-day expedited transition, and costs borne by regulated entity.

risk_patterns.name: No Crisis Communication Plan Between Parties
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical service contract without crisis communication plan and escalation contacts
risk_patterns.description: During major incidents, inability to reach decision-makers delays response and increases impact.
risk_patterns.impact: Delayed crisis response from inability to reach authorised decision-makers.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for crisis communication: 24/7 escalation contacts, war room procedures, executive escalation, and communication channels.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include crisis communication plan: 24/7 contacts (updated quarterly), war room activation criteria, executive bridge within 1 hour, and secure communication channel.

risk_patterns.name: Healthcare Patient Data Continuity Gap
risk_patterns.trigger: Healthcare IT contract without patient data continuity and access guarantees during system failures
risk_patterns.description: Healthcare system failures must not prevent clinical access to patient data; downtime procedures are essential.
risk_patterns.impact: Patient safety risk from inability to access clinical records during system failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clinical data continuity: downtime procedures, read-only access mode, clinical safety case, and paper fallback.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require downtime procedures, read-only access within 15 minutes of failure, local clinical data cache, and tested paper-based fallback procedures.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: NHS WannaCry attack caused cancellation of 19,000 appointments and diversion of emergency patients.

risk_patterns.name: No Supply Chain Cyber Resilience
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply chain contract without cyber resilience requirements for critical suppliers
risk_patterns.description: NIS2 requires supply chain cybersecurity risk management; supplier compromise can cascade to customer operations.
risk_patterns.impact: Operational disruption from supplier cyber incident; supply chain cascade failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for supplier cyber requirements: security baseline, incident notification, vulnerability management, and access controls.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require supplier cyber baseline (ISO 27001 or Cyber Essentials Plus), 24-hour incident notification, vulnerability patching SLAs, and annual assessment.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: SolarWinds and Kaseya supply chain attacks demonstrated catastrophic cascade effects.

risk_patterns.name: No Noise Pollution Controls
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction or industrial operations contract without noise pollution limits and monitoring
risk_patterns.description: Noise complaints can result in injunctions, planning enforcement, and project delays.
risk_patterns.impact: Project injunctions and planning enforcement from noise limit breaches.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For construction or industrial contracts, check for noise limits, monitoring requirements, working hours restrictions, and complaint procedures.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include noise management plan: defined limits (dB at boundary), monitoring requirements, restricted working hours, and complaint response procedure.

risk_patterns.name: No Asbestos Management Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: Building maintenance or renovation contract without asbestos management provisions
risk_patterns.description: Disturbing asbestos-containing materials without management plan creates serious health risks and criminal liability.
risk_patterns.impact: Criminal prosecution under CAR 2012; site closure; health claims.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For works on pre-2000 buildings, check for asbestos survey requirement, management plan, and licensed removal contractor obligation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require asbestos survey before works, management plan for identified ACMs, licensed removal contractor for notifiable work, and air monitoring.

risk_patterns.name: No Soil Contamination Assessment
risk_patterns.trigger: Land transaction without soil contamination assessment for brownfield or industrial sites
risk_patterns.description: Soil contamination on brownfield sites creates remediation liability for the landowner.
risk_patterns.impact: Remediation costs of EUR 200K-10M for contaminated soil on industrial sites.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For land transactions on brownfield sites, check for soil investigation, contamination assessment, and liability allocation for existing contamination.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require soil investigation before completion, detailed contamination assessment, seller indemnity for pre-existing contamination, and remediation cost cap.

risk_patterns.name: No Air Quality Compliance Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Manufacturing or logistics contract without air quality and emissions compliance requirements
risk_patterns.description: Industrial emissions must comply with environmental permits; contractual obligations ensure compliance by operators.
risk_patterns.impact: Environmental permit breach; enforcement notice; operational shutdown.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For manufacturing contracts, check for emission limits, permit compliance, monitoring, and reporting obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include air quality clause: compliance with environmental permit, continuous emission monitoring, annual reporting, and breach notification.

risk_patterns.name: No Renewable Energy Sourcing Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Facility management or data centre contract without renewable energy sourcing requirements
risk_patterns.description: Corporate RE100 commitments and regulatory expectations require contracted services to use renewable energy.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to meet corporate renewable energy commitments; Scope 2 emissions increase.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For energy-intensive service contracts, check for renewable energy sourcing requirements, REGOs/GOs, and progress targets.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require minimum 50% renewable energy (increasing to 100% by 2030), backed by REGOs or Guarantees of Origin, with annual evidence.

risk_patterns.name: No EU Data Act Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: IoT or connected product contract without EU Data Act data sharing obligations
risk_patterns.description: EU Data Act requires manufacturers to provide users access to product-generated data and enable third-party sharing.
risk_patterns.impact: Fines and injunctions for non-compliance with Data Act data access obligations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For IoT or connected product contracts, check for Data Act compliance: user data access, third-party sharing, and interoperability.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include Data Act compliance clause: user data access in real-time, third-party sharing capability, interoperability requirements, and fair contract terms.

risk_patterns.name: No Unfair Trading Practices Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Food supply chain contract without unfair trading practices directive compliance
risk_patterns.description: EU Directive 2019/633 prohibits specific unfair trading practices in agricultural and food supply chains.
risk_patterns.impact: Regulatory enforcement; contract terms voided as unfair trading practices.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For food supply contracts, check for compliance with UTP Directive: payment terms, cancellation rights, and prohibited practices.
risk_patterns.remediation: Ensure compliance with UTP Directive: 30/60 day payment terms, no last-minute cancellations, no unilateral changes, and no return of unsold goods at supplier cost.

risk_patterns.name: No Foreign Subsidy Regulation Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Large EU procurement or M&A transaction without EU Foreign Subsidy Regulation assessment
risk_patterns.description: EU FSR requires notification for large subsidised acquisitions and procurement bids in the EU.
risk_patterns.impact: Transaction blocked or unwound for non-notification; financial penalties.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For M&A >EUR 500M or EU procurements >EUR 250M, check for FSR notification assessment and filing obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include FSR compliance clause: notification assessment, filing cooperation, information provision, and standstill period acknowledgment.

risk_patterns.name: No DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial services ICT contract without DORA-mandated threat-led penetration testing provisions
risk_patterns.description: DORA requires threat-led penetration testing (TLPT) for critical ICT services at least every 3 years.
risk_patterns.impact: DORA non-compliance; regulatory criticism of untested operational resilience.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical financial services ICT, check for TLPT obligations: testing scope, red team participation, joint testing, and remediation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include TLPT clause: triennial testing minimum, scope to include provider systems, joint red team exercises, 30-day remediation for critical findings.

risk_patterns.name: No NIST AI RMF Alignment for US Operations
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system contract for US market without NIST AI Risk Management Framework alignment
risk_patterns.description: NIST AI RMF is the de facto US standard for AI risk management; alignment expected for government and enterprise contracts.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to meet US customer AI governance expectations; competitive disadvantage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For AI contracts targeting US market, check for NIST AI RMF alignment: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include NIST AI RMF alignment: risk governance structure, AI system mapping, performance measurement, and ongoing risk management.

risk_patterns.name: No Platform-to-Business Regulation Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Platform services contract without P2B Regulation compliance for business users
risk_patterns.description: EU P2B Regulation requires platforms to provide transparent terms, ranking criteria, and complaint mechanisms to business users.
risk_patterns.impact: Void contract terms; regulatory enforcement; business user complaints.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For platform service contracts with business users, check for P2B compliance: transparent terms, ranking transparency, and complaint mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include P2B compliance: plain language terms, ranking criteria disclosure, 15-day notice of changes, and internal complaint handling system.

risk_patterns.name: No Anti-Fraud Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: Financial or procurement contract without anti-fraud controls and investigation cooperation
risk_patterns.description: Fraud losses are significant across industries; contractual anti-fraud provisions enable detection and recovery.
risk_patterns.impact: Undetected fraud; inability to recover fraudulent payments; regulatory criticism.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for anti-fraud provisions: anti-fraud representation, investigation cooperation, clawback rights, and fraud reporting obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include anti-fraud clause: zero-tolerance representation, investigation cooperation, clawback rights for fraudulent payments, and whistleblower channel.

risk_patterns.name: No Consumer Credit Regulation Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: Buy-now-pay-later or embedded finance contract without consumer credit regulation compliance
risk_patterns.description: BNPL and embedded finance increasingly fall within consumer credit regulation scope.
risk_patterns.impact: Unenforceable credit agreements; FCA enforcement; consumer redress.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For BNPL or embedded finance, check for consumer credit compliance: FCA authorisation, pre-contractual information, and affordability assessment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require FCA authorisation verification, pre-contractual information compliance, affordability assessment, and persistent debt management.

risk_patterns.name: No Transfer Pricing Documentation
risk_patterns.trigger: Intra-group service agreement without transfer pricing documentation and arm's-length pricing
risk_patterns.description: Transfer pricing non-compliance creates double taxation, penalties, and criminal liability in severe cases.
risk_patterns.impact: Double taxation, penalties of 20-40% of underpaid tax, and reputational damage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For intra-group contracts, check for transfer pricing documentation: benchmarking study, master file, local file, and CbCR.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require arm's-length pricing with benchmarking study, maintain master file and local file, update annually, and include advance pricing agreement consideration.

risk_patterns.name: No Consumer ADR Information
risk_patterns.trigger: B2C contract without information about alternative dispute resolution per EU ADR Directive
risk_patterns.description: EU ADR Directive requires traders to inform consumers about available ADR entities.
risk_patterns.impact: Trading standards enforcement; consumer complaints about lack of ADR information.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For B2C contracts, check for ADR information: named ADR entity, ODR platform link, and commitment to participate.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include ADR information: named ADR entity, link to EU ODR platform, and statement on willingness to engage in ADR.

risk_patterns.name: Unchecked Standard Form Terms
risk_patterns.trigger: Counterparty standard form terms accepted without negotiation or review
risk_patterns.description: Vendor standard forms contain one-sided provisions that accumulate significant hidden risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Accumulated one-sided risk from unreviewed standard form provisions.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Flag acceptance of counterparty standard terms without review. Check for: unilateral amendment, unlimited liability, IP assignment, and auto-renewal.
risk_patterns.remediation: Always review counterparty standard terms. Prioritise: liability caps, IP ownership, termination rights, data protection, and amendment provisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Conditions Precedent for Commitment
risk_patterns.trigger: Binding commitment made without conditions precedent for material uncertainties
risk_patterns.description: Binding without conditions precedent locks parties in regardless of due diligence findings or regulatory approvals.
risk_patterns.impact: Forced completion despite material adverse findings or missing regulatory approvals.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for conditions precedent: regulatory approval, satisfactory due diligence, financing, and material adverse change.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include conditions precedent for: regulatory approvals, satisfactory DD, financing confirmation, and no MAC. Define long-stop date and termination rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Material Adverse Change Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A or investment agreement without MAC clause allowing exit for fundamental changes
risk_patterns.description: Without MAC clause, buyer must complete even if target's business fundamentally deteriorates between signing and closing.
risk_patterns.impact: Forced completion of deteriorated acquisition at original price.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for MAC clause: definition scope, carve-outs, materiality threshold, and measurement period.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include MAC clause with broad definition, narrow carve-outs, defined materiality threshold (typically 10-20% of value), and look-back period.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: COVID-19 triggered numerous MAC disputes in M&A transactions.

risk_patterns.name: No Anti-Dilution Protection for Investors
risk_patterns.trigger: Investment agreement without anti-dilution protection for down-round financing
risk_patterns.description: Without anti-dilution, investors lose proportional ownership in subsequent down-round funding events.
risk_patterns.impact: Significant ownership dilution from down-round financing without price protection.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for anti-dilution provisions: weighted average or full ratchet, exceptions for employee options, and pay-to-play requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include broad-based weighted average anti-dilution, with exceptions for employee option pool (up to 15%), and pay-to-play provisions.

risk_patterns.name: No Deadlock Resolution Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Joint venture or 50/50 partnership without deadlock resolution mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Equal partnerships without deadlock resolution become paralysed when shareholders disagree.
risk_patterns.impact: Business paralysis from unresolvable shareholder deadlock.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For 50/50 JVs or equal partnerships, check for deadlock mechanism: escalation, mediation, buy-sell, Russian roulette, or winding up.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include tiered deadlock resolution: CEO escalation (14 days), mediation (30 days), then buy-sell mechanism with fair value determination.

risk_patterns.name: No Tag-Along / Drag-Along Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Shareholders agreement without tag-along and drag-along provisions for exit
risk_patterns.description: Without tag-along, minority shareholders are left behind in exit; without drag-along, majority cannot compel full exit.
risk_patterns.impact: Minority shareholders trapped or majority unable to deliver 100% exit to buyer.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for tag-along (minority follow majority) and drag-along (majority compel minority) provisions with fair value protections.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include tag-along rights at same price and terms, drag-along rights with fair value floor, and 30-day notice for both mechanisms.

risk_patterns.name: No Reserved Matters for Minority Protection
risk_patterns.trigger: Joint venture or minority investment without reserved matters requiring unanimous consent
risk_patterns.description: Without reserved matters, majority can take fundamental decisions without minority consent.
risk_patterns.impact: Minority interests prejudiced by majority decisions on fundamental matters.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for reserved matters: constitutional changes, major transactions, related party dealings, new share issuance, and business plan changes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include reserved matters list: constitutional changes, transactions >10% of NAV, related party dealings, share issuance, and material litigation.

risk_patterns.name: No Pre-Emption Rights on Share Transfer
risk_patterns.trigger: Shareholders agreement without pre-emption rights giving existing shareholders first refusal
risk_patterns.description: Without pre-emption rights, shares can be transferred to unwanted third parties without shareholder consent.
risk_patterns.impact: Unwanted third-party shareholders; loss of control over shareholder composition.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for pre-emption rights: right of first refusal, fair value determination, timeframe for exercise, and permitted transfers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include pre-emption rights: 30-day ROFR at fair value, independent valuation mechanism, and permitted transfers to affiliates only.

risk_patterns.name: No Investor Information Rights
risk_patterns.trigger: Investment agreement without investor information and inspection rights
risk_patterns.description: Without information rights, investors cannot monitor their investment or exercise governance rights effectively.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to monitor investment; late discovery of financial problems.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for information rights: monthly management accounts, annual audited accounts, budget approval, and inspection rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include information rights: monthly management accounts within 15 days, annual audited accounts within 90 days, annual budget approval, and inspection rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Good Leaver / Bad Leaver Mechanism
risk_patterns.trigger: Founder or management equity agreement without good leaver/bad leaver distinction
risk_patterns.description: Without leaver provisions, departed founders retain full equity regardless of circumstances of departure.
risk_patterns.impact: Departed founders retaining equity value; inability to reallocate to replacement team.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for leaver provisions: good leaver (fair value), bad leaver (lower of cost/fair value), and vesting schedule.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include good leaver (fair value) and bad leaver (lower of cost/nominal value) provisions, 4-year vesting with 1-year cliff, and board discretion.

risk_patterns.name: No M&A Holdback for Warranty Claims
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction without escrow or holdback mechanism for warranty claims
risk_patterns.description: Without holdback, buyer has no security for warranty claims and must pursue seller personally.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to recover warranty claims if seller dissipates sale proceeds.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for escrow or holdback: amount (typically 10-15% of price), duration (18-24 months), and release mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include 10-15% escrow or holdback for 18-24 months, joint release instructions, and automatic release for unclaimed amounts at expiry.

risk_patterns.name: No Working Capital Adjustment in M&A
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A completion mechanism without working capital target and adjustment
risk_patterns.description: Without working capital adjustment, seller can drain working capital before completion, leaving buyer cash-short.
risk_patterns.impact: Working capital shortfall requiring immediate cash injection post-completion.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for working capital target, measurement methodology, adjustment mechanism, and dispute resolution for WC calculations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include working capital target based on 12-month average, EUR-for-EUR adjustment, agreed measurement methodology, and expert determination for disputes.

risk_patterns.name: No Break Fee for Transaction Costs
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction without break fee to compensate for aborted deal costs
risk_patterns.description: Without break fees, parties bear full transaction costs if counterparty walks away from the deal.
risk_patterns.impact: Unrecoverable transaction costs of EUR 100K-2M for aborted deals.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for break fee provisions: trigger events, amount (typically 1-3% of deal value), and reverse break fee for buyer walkaway.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include break fee of 1-3% of deal value payable by party causing deal failure, with carve-outs for regulatory non-approval.

risk_patterns.name: Construction Final Account Dispute Risk
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction contract without clear final account preparation and agreement process
risk_patterns.description: Final account disputes are the most common construction disputes, often lasting years after practical completion.
risk_patterns.impact: Multi-year disputes over final account sums; legal costs exceeding disputed amounts.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for final account process: submission deadline, review period, deemed agreement, and dispute mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include final account process: submission within 6 months of PC, 3-month review, deemed agreement if not disputed, and adjudication for disputes.

risk_patterns.name: No W&I Insurance Assessment for M&A
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction without assessment of warranty and indemnity insurance availability
risk_patterns.description: W&I insurance can bridge warranty risk gaps, particularly with financial buyers or limited seller recourse.
risk_patterns.impact: Uninsured warranty risk where seller recourse is limited.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if W&I insurance has been assessed: insurable risks, excluded risks, policy limits, and premium allocation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Assess W&I insurance availability, understand excluded risks, align warranty package with insurability, and include premium allocation in price.

risk_patterns.name: No Retention of Title Clause
risk_patterns.trigger: Goods supply contract without retention of title clause protecting seller
risk_patterns.description: Without retention of title, seller becomes unsecured creditor if buyer becomes insolvent after delivery but before payment.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of delivered goods value if buyer becomes insolvent before payment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For goods supply contracts, check for retention of title: simple ROT, all-monies ROT, and registration requirements.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include all-monies retention of title: ownership retained until all sums paid, right to repossess, and buyer holds as bailee. Register if required.

risk_patterns.name: No Financial Relief During Force Majeure
risk_patterns.trigger: Force majeure clause excusing performance but without addressing payment obligations during the event
risk_patterns.description: Force majeure may excuse delivery but not payment, creating financial strain during extended FM events.
risk_patterns.impact: Continuing payment obligations during extended force majeure periods.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if force majeure addresses financial obligations: payment suspension, pro-rata adjustment, and cost sharing during FM period.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include financial provisions for FM: suspend affected payments, pro-rate for partial performance, share unavoidable costs, and quarterly review.

risk_patterns.name: No Performance Bond for Advance Payment
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract requiring advance payment without advance payment bond or guarantee
risk_patterns.description: Advance payments without bonds expose buyer to total loss if contractor fails to deliver.
risk_patterns.impact: Total loss of advance payment on contractor default.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if advance payments >10% of contract value are secured by advance payment bond from A-rated surety.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require advance payment bond for advances >10% of contract value, from A-rated surety, reducing proportionally as work is performed.

risk_patterns.name: No Interim Payment Mechanism for Long Projects
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-duration project contract without interim or progress payment mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Without interim payments, contractor must finance all work until completion, creating cash flow and insolvency risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Contractor cash flow failure; project abandonment from financing inability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >3 months, check for interim payment mechanism: monthly valuations, payment cycle, and certification process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include monthly interim payments: application by 20th, certification within 5 days, payment within 14 days, and pay less notice process.

risk_patterns.name: No Cap on Delay Damages
risk_patterns.trigger: Construction or delivery contract with uncapped delay damages
risk_patterns.description: Uncapped delay damages can accumulate beyond the contract value during extended delays.
risk_patterns.impact: Delay damages exceeding contract value; project becoming loss-making from delays alone.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check delay damages: rate per day/week, aggregate cap (typically 10-15% of contract value), and termination right at cap.
risk_patterns.remediation: Cap delay damages at 10-15% of contract value, with termination right once cap reached, and deduction from interim payments.

risk_patterns.name: No Separate Testing Environment
risk_patterns.trigger: SaaS or software contract without provision for staging or testing environment
risk_patterns.description: Without testing environment, changes must be tested in production, creating outage and data corruption risk.
risk_patterns.impact: Production incidents from untested changes; data corruption from testing in live environment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for testing environment: separate staging instance, data refresh capability, and pre-production testing obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require separate staging environment with representative data, pre-production testing for all changes, and customer UAT rights.

risk_patterns.name: No Release Management Process
risk_patterns.trigger: Software service contract without defined release management and deployment process
risk_patterns.description: Ad-hoc releases without process create instability and unplanned customer impact.
risk_patterns.impact: Production instability from uncontrolled releases; unplanned customer disruption.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for release management: release calendar, notification period, rollback capability, and change freeze periods.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include release management: monthly release calendar, 5-day advance notification, rollback within 1 hour, and quarterly change freeze periods.

risk_patterns.name: No SLA Measurement Methodology Defined
risk_patterns.trigger: SLA with uptime targets but no defined measurement methodology or exclusions
risk_patterns.description: Without defined measurement, SLA disputes arise over calculation methodology, exclusion windows, and monitoring points.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed SLA performance due to undefined measurement methodology.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check SLA measurement: monitoring points, calculation method, exclusion windows, and reporting frequency.
risk_patterns.remediation: Define SLA measurement: monitoring from customer gateway, 5-minute polling, exclude planned maintenance (max 4 hours/month), and monthly reports within 5 days.

risk_patterns.name: No Patch Management SLA
risk_patterns.trigger: Software or infrastructure contract without defined patch management and vulnerability remediation SLAs
risk_patterns.description: Without patch SLAs, critical vulnerabilities remain unpatched for extended periods.
risk_patterns.impact: Exploitation of unpatched vulnerabilities; breach liability from known vulnerability non-remediation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for patch SLAs: critical (24 hours), high (72 hours), medium (30 days), low (90 days), and emergency patch process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include patch SLAs: critical within 24 hours, high within 72 hours, medium within 30 days, and emergency out-of-cycle patch capability.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Equifax breach resulted from unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability known for 2 months.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Dependency Documentation
risk_patterns.trigger: Complex service delivery without documentation of service dependencies and integration points
risk_patterns.description: Undocumented dependencies create cascade failures and make impact assessment impossible.
