risk_patterns.impact: Cascade failures from unknown dependencies; inability to assess change impact.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for service dependency documentation: architecture diagram, integration points, dependency map, and impact assessment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require service dependency documentation: architecture diagram, integration map, quarterly review, and impact assessment for all changes.

risk_patterns.name: No Backup Restoration Testing
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract with backup requirements but no restoration testing obligations
risk_patterns.description: Backups without restoration testing may be corrupt or incomplete, discovered only during actual recovery.
risk_patterns.impact: Failed recovery from untested backups during actual disaster.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for restoration testing: frequency (minimum quarterly), scope (full and partial), success criteria, and customer notification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require quarterly backup restoration testing: full system restore, data integrity verification, published results, and remediation for failures.

risk_patterns.name: No Access Management Controls for Provider
risk_patterns.trigger: Managed service contract without access management controls for provider personnel
risk_patterns.description: Without access controls, provider personnel have excessive access to customer systems and data.
risk_patterns.impact: Insider threat risk; excessive access leading to data breach or sabotage.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for access management: least privilege, MFA, privileged access management, access reviews, and audit logging.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require least privilege access, MFA for all access, PAM for administrative access, quarterly access reviews, and comprehensive audit logging.

risk_patterns.name: No End-of-Life Hardware Management
risk_patterns.trigger: Infrastructure service contract without hardware end-of-life replacement obligations
risk_patterns.description: EOL hardware loses vendor support, creating security vulnerabilities and reliability risks.
risk_patterns.impact: Security vulnerabilities from unsupported hardware; increased failure rates.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for hardware lifecycle management: EOS/EOL tracking, replacement schedule, and customer notification for lifecycle changes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include hardware lifecycle clause: EOS notification 12 months in advance, replacement before EOL, and no production use of EOL hardware.

risk_patterns.name: No Ongoing Compliance Monitoring
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract with compliance requirements but no ongoing monitoring or verification mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Point-in-time compliance assessments miss drift and degradation between audit cycles.
risk_patterns.impact: Compliance drift between audits; non-compliance discovered only at next assessment.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for compliance monitoring: continuous monitoring tools, quarterly self-assessments, and automated compliance checking.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include continuous compliance monitoring: automated checking where possible, quarterly self-assessments, and annual independent audit.

risk_patterns.name: No Service Catalogue Maintenance
risk_patterns.trigger: Managed service contract without maintained service catalogue defining all in-scope services
risk_patterns.description: Without maintained service catalogue, scope disputes arise over what is included in the service fees.
risk_patterns.impact: Ongoing scope disputes; out-of-scope charges for services customer considers included.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for service catalogue: defined in-scope services, exclusions, update process, and customer approval for changes.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include maintained service catalogue as schedule: all services listed, quarterly review, mutual approval for changes, and clear in-scope/out-of-scope delineation.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Classification Framework in Contract
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract handling multiple data types without classification framework defining handling requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without data classification, all data receives the same protection level, either over-protected (costly) or under-protected (risky).
risk_patterns.impact: Inappropriate protection levels; security resources misallocated across data types.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data classification: categories (public, internal, confidential, restricted), handling requirements per category, and labelling.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include data classification framework: 4 levels with defined handling, encryption, access, and retention requirements per classification.

risk_patterns.name: No Secure Data Destruction Requirements
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without secure data destruction requirements and certification
risk_patterns.description: Improper data destruction leaves recoverable data on media, creating breach risk after contract termination.
risk_patterns.impact: Data recovery from improperly destroyed media; post-termination data breach.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data destruction: NIST 800-88 or equivalent standard, certification of destruction, and media handling for different classification levels.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require NIST 800-88 compliant destruction, certificate of destruction within 30 days, witnessed destruction for highly classified data, and media inventory.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple data breaches have resulted from improper disposal of storage media.

risk_patterns.name: No Privacy by Design in Development Contracts
risk_patterns.trigger: Software development contract without privacy by design and default requirements
risk_patterns.description: GDPR Article 25 requires privacy by design; development contracts must embed this into the delivery process.
risk_patterns.impact: Costly post-deployment privacy remediation; GDPR Article 25 non-compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for PbD requirements: privacy requirements in specifications, data minimisation, purpose limitation, and privacy testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include PbD clause: privacy requirements in each sprint, data minimisation review, privacy testing before release, and DPIA for new features.

risk_patterns.name: No Third-Party Data Sharing Controls
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract allowing data sharing with third parties without defined controls and restrictions
risk_patterns.description: Uncontrolled third-party data sharing expands the attack surface and creates cascading GDPR compliance obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Unauthorised data sharing; cascading GDPR breaches across third-party chain.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for third-party sharing controls: permitted recipients, purpose restrictions, contractual flow-down, and notification obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Restrict third-party sharing to permitted recipients, require contractual flow-down of data protection obligations, and notify controller of sharing requests.

risk_patterns.name: No Cross-Service Data Segregation
risk_patterns.trigger: Multi-service provider contract without data segregation requirements between service lines
risk_patterns.description: Provider handling data for multiple services may commingle data without proper segregation controls.
risk_patterns.impact: Data accessed by unauthorised service teams; purpose limitation violations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For multi-service providers, check for data segregation: logical/physical separation, access controls between services, and audit trail.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require data segregation between service lines: logical separation, separate access controls, no cross-service data access, and segregation audit.

risk_patterns.name: No DNS Resilience for Critical Services
risk_patterns.trigger: Internet-facing service without DNS resilience and failover provisions
risk_patterns.description: DNS failures can render all services unreachable regardless of infrastructure availability.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete service unreachability from DNS provider failure.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for DNS resilience: multiple DNS providers, geographic distribution, TTL management, and failover testing.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require multiple DNS providers, sub-5-minute failover, geographic distribution, and annual DNS failover testing.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Dyn DNS attack in 2016 took down major internet services including Twitter, Netflix, and GitHub.

risk_patterns.name: No TLS Certificate Management
risk_patterns.trigger: Web service contract without TLS certificate management and renewal provisions
risk_patterns.description: Certificate expiry causes complete service outage; automated management prevents this common failure mode.
risk_patterns.impact: Service outage and security warnings from expired certificates.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for certificate management: automated renewal, monitoring, expiry alerting, and certificate authority redundancy.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require automated certificate management, 30-day expiry alerts, monitoring for all certificates, and CA redundancy.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Multiple major outages (Microsoft Teams, Spotify) caused by expired TLS certificates.

risk_patterns.name: No Capacity Planning for Anticipated Growth
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without capacity planning process aligned to business growth forecasts
risk_patterns.description: Without capacity planning, growth outstrips infrastructure capacity causing degradation and outages.
risk_patterns.impact: Performance degradation and outages during growth phases.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for capacity planning: quarterly forecasting, capacity thresholds, lead times for scaling, and proactive monitoring.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include capacity planning: quarterly forecast review, 70% utilisation threshold trigger for expansion, and defined lead times for scaling.

risk_patterns.name: No Incident Response Playbooks for Common Scenarios
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract without documented incident response playbooks for common failure scenarios
risk_patterns.description: Without playbooks, incident response is ad-hoc, slower, and more error-prone.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended incident duration from ad-hoc response; inconsistent handling of recurring scenarios.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for incident playbooks: top 10 failure scenarios, response procedures, escalation criteria, and customer communication templates.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require documented playbooks for top 10 failure scenarios, annual review, tabletop exercises, and customer communication templates.

risk_patterns.name: No Third-Party Service Monitoring
risk_patterns.trigger: Service dependent on third-party components without monitoring and failover provisions
risk_patterns.description: Third-party service failures cascade to dependent services without monitoring and automatic failover.
risk_patterns.impact: Cascade outages from unmonitored third-party dependencies.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for third-party monitoring: dependency identification, health monitoring, automatic failover, and degraded-mode operation.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require third-party dependency map, real-time health monitoring, automatic failover where possible, and documented degraded-mode procedures.

risk_patterns.name: No Escrow for Critical Deposits
risk_patterns.trigger: High-value transaction requiring deposit without escrow arrangement
risk_patterns.description: Deposits paid directly to counterparty are at risk if counterparty becomes insolvent before performance.
risk_patterns.impact: Total loss of deposit on counterparty insolvency.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For deposits >EUR 100K, check for escrow arrangement with independent custodian and defined release conditions.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require escrow for deposits >EUR 100K: independent custodian, defined release conditions, interest allocation, and dispute hold mechanism.

risk_patterns.name: No Cost of Living Adjustment for Employment
risk_patterns.trigger: Employment contract without cost of living adjustment or salary review mechanism
risk_patterns.description: Without COLA or review mechanism, real wages decline over time leading to retention issues.
risk_patterns.impact: Real wage erosion leading to increased attrition and recruitment costs.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check employment contracts for annual salary review mechanism, COLA adjustment, or performance-based increment process.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include annual salary review: minimum CPI adjustment, performance increment opportunity, and transparent review criteria.

risk_patterns.name: No Documentation Standards for Deliverables
risk_patterns.trigger: Service or development contract without documentation standards and delivery requirements
risk_patterns.description: Without documentation standards, deliverables lack the documentation needed for maintenance and handover.
risk_patterns.impact: Undocumented systems creating maintenance dependency and knowledge loss.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for documentation requirements: technical docs, user guides, API documentation, architecture diagrams, and runbooks.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include documentation requirements: technical architecture docs, API documentation (OpenAPI), user guides, runbooks, and acceptance criteria for docs.

risk_patterns.name: No Capacity Reservation for Peak Periods
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract with known peak periods without reserved capacity provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without reserved capacity, service degrades during predictable peak periods when resources are shared.
risk_patterns.impact: Service degradation during critical business peak periods.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for peak capacity provisions: reserved capacity, burst provisions, and SLA modifications during known peak periods.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include peak capacity reservation: define peak periods, reserve 150% of baseline capacity, and SLA applies equally during peak.

risk_patterns.name: No Supply Chain Due Diligence (CSDDD)
risk_patterns.trigger: Supply chain contract without corporate sustainability due diligence obligations per EU CSDDD
risk_patterns.description: EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive requires supply chain human rights and environmental due diligence.
risk_patterns.impact: CSDDD non-compliance fines up to 5% of global turnover; civil liability for harm.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For material suppliers, check for CSDDD provisions: human rights due diligence, environmental due diligence, and remediation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include CSDDD provisions: human rights assessment, environmental due diligence, corrective action plans, and termination as last resort.

risk_patterns.name: No Digital Euro Preparation
risk_patterns.trigger: Payment services contract without provisions for future digital euro acceptance requirements
risk_patterns.description: EU digital euro regulation will require basic merchants to accept digital euro payments.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with digital euro acceptance obligation when enacted.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For payment service contracts, check for future-proofing: digital euro readiness clause, technology update obligations, and regulatory change mechanism.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include regulatory future-proofing clause: technology update for digital euro when required, cost allocation, and implementation timeline.

risk_patterns.name: No Anti-Counterfeiting Provisions
risk_patterns.trigger: Product supply contract without anti-counterfeiting measures and verification obligations
risk_patterns.description: Counterfeit products in supply chains create safety, regulatory, and reputational risks.
risk_patterns.impact: Product recalls; regulatory penalties; personal injury liability from counterfeit components.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For product supply contracts, check for anti-counterfeiting: authentication measures, traceability, testing, and indemnification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include anti-counterfeiting clause: authentication measures, serialisation/traceability, incoming goods testing, and counterfeit component indemnification.

risk_patterns.name: No Limitation on Recourse for Non-Recourse Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Project finance or SPV contract without clear non-recourse provisions
risk_patterns.description: Without non-recourse language, sponsors may face unexpected personal liability for SPV obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Personal liability of sponsors for SPV debts and obligations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For SPV or project finance structures, check for non-recourse provisions, carve-outs, and comfort letter boundaries.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clear non-recourse provision: obligations limited to SPV assets, defined carve-outs (fraud, misrepresentation), and no pierce of corporate veil.

risk_patterns.name: No Controls on Powers of Attorney
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract granting power of attorney without scope limitations, expiry, and revocation rights
risk_patterns.description: Broad PoA grants without controls allow agent to bind principal to unintended obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Principal bound to obligations not authorised or intended.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for PoA controls: defined scope, expiry date, revocation rights, and reporting obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Limit PoA to specific transactions, include automatic expiry date, require monthly reporting, and preserve immediate revocation right.

risk_patterns.name: No AI Model Ownership Allocation
risk_patterns.trigger: Custom AI model development contract without ownership allocation for trained models
risk_patterns.description: Custom-trained AI models represent significant investment; ownership must be explicitly allocated.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed ownership of custom-trained models costing EUR 100K-1M+ to develop.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for model ownership: base model vs fine-tuned weights, training data contribution, and model portability rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Customer owns fine-tuned model weights and training data contributions. Provider retains base model. Include model export rights in standard formats.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Residency for AI Inference Processing
risk_patterns.trigger: AI service contract where inference API calls may be routed to non-EU data centres
risk_patterns.description: AI inference requests containing personal data may be processed in non-EU locations without adequate safeguards.
risk_patterns.impact: GDPR violations from personal data in inference requests processed outside EEA.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check if AI inference API requests are processed exclusively in EU. Verify data flow for prompts, completions, and logging.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require EU-only inference processing, contractual data residency guarantee, no prompt logging outside EU, and data flow documentation.

risk_patterns.name: No Warm Standby for Critical Applications
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical application contract with cold DR only (no warm standby or active-passive failover)
risk_patterns.description: Cold DR requires hours of recovery time; critical applications need warm standby for rapid failover.
risk_patterns.impact: RTO of 4-24 hours for cold DR vs minutes for warm standby on critical applications.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For critical applications (RTO <1 hour), check for warm standby or active-passive architecture with automated failover.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require warm standby with automated failover for critical applications: <15-minute RTO, <5-minute RPO, and quarterly failover testing.

risk_patterns.name: No Social Media Crisis Response Protocol
risk_patterns.trigger: Brand-facing service contract without social media crisis response coordination
risk_patterns.description: Service incidents amplified on social media require coordinated response within minutes, not hours.
risk_patterns.impact: Viral reputational damage from uncoordinated social media response to service incidents.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for social media crisis protocol: monitoring, coordinated response, approved messaging, and escalation triggers.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include social media protocol: real-time monitoring, coordinated response within 30 minutes, pre-approved holding statements, and executive escalation.

risk_patterns.name: No Biodiversity Net Gain Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Development contract in England without mandatory 10% biodiversity net gain from February 2024
risk_patterns.description: Environment Act 2021 requires 10% biodiversity net gain for most developments in England.
risk_patterns.impact: Planning refusal or enforcement from failure to achieve mandatory BNG.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For England developments, check for BNG plan: baseline assessment, 10% net gain calculation, 30-year management plan, and monitoring.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include BNG clause: pre-development baseline, 10% net gain plan, 30-year management and monitoring, and financial security for long-term management.

risk_patterns.name: No Restitution Provisions on Contract Rescission
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without provisions for restitution and unwinding if the contract is rescinded
risk_patterns.description: Rescission requires parties to return to pre-contract position; without provisions, unwinding is costly and disputed.
risk_patterns.impact: Disputed unwinding costs and incomplete restitution on contract rescission.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for rescission/unwinding provisions: return of property, payment adjustments, IP reversion, and confidential information return.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include unwinding provisions: mutual return of property within 30 days, pro-rata payment adjustment, IP reversion, and data return/destruction.

risk_patterns.name: No End-User Support Standards
risk_patterns.trigger: Software service contract without defined end-user support standards and escalation
risk_patterns.description: Without support standards, users experience inconsistent quality and long resolution times.
risk_patterns.impact: User productivity loss from poor support; user adoption decline.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for support standards: channels (phone, email, chat), hours, response times, resolution targets, and escalation path.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include support standards: multi-channel support, business hours minimum, P1 response in 15 minutes, 80% first-call resolution, and escalation path.

risk_patterns.name: No SOX Controls for US-Listed Entity
risk_patterns.trigger: Service contract with US-listed entity without SOX-relevant internal control provisions
risk_patterns.description: US-listed entities must ensure service providers support SOX internal control requirements.
risk_patterns.impact: SOX material weakness; SEC enforcement; auditor qualification.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts with US-listed entities, check for SOX provisions: internal controls, SOC 1/2 reporting, and access to audit evidence.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include SOX clause: maintain internal controls, provide annual SOC 1 Type II report, cooperate with external auditors, and report control failures.

risk_patterns.name: No Zero-Day Vulnerability Response Capability
risk_patterns.trigger: Critical infrastructure contract without zero-day vulnerability response provisions
risk_patterns.description: Zero-day vulnerabilities require emergency response outside normal patch management cycles.
risk_patterns.impact: Extended exposure to actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for zero-day response: emergency patch capability, temporary mitigation deployment, and communication protocol.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require zero-day response: 4-hour mitigation for critical zero-days, emergency patch within 48 hours, and customer notification within 1 hour.
risk_patterns.real_world_impact: Log4Shell, MOVEit, and other zero-days required emergency response outside normal patch cycles.

risk_patterns.name: No GDPR One-Stop-Shop Coordination
risk_patterns.trigger: Cross-border processing arrangement without coordination of lead supervisory authority
risk_patterns.description: Cross-border processing requires identification of lead DPA for consistency mechanism coordination.
risk_patterns.impact: Multi-DPA investigations and conflicting decisions from uncoordinated regulatory engagement.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For cross-border processing, check for lead DPA identification, main establishment documentation, and regulatory cooperation obligations.
risk_patterns.remediation: Identify lead DPA based on main establishment, document basis for determination, and include cooperation obligations for regulatory inquiries.

risk_patterns.name: No Clawback for Material Misrepresentation
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without clawback mechanism for payments made based on material misrepresentations
risk_patterns.description: Without clawback rights, payments made based on false representations cannot be efficiently recovered.
risk_patterns.impact: Inability to recover payments made based on false information without litigation.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for clawback provisions: trigger events (misrepresentation, fraud), recovery mechanism, timeframe, and set-off rights.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include clawback clause: automatic right to recover payments made based on material misrepresentation, set-off against future payments, and interest.

risk_patterns.name: No IP-Specific Governing Law Consideration
risk_patterns.trigger: IP-intensive international contract without consideration of IP-specific jurisdictional issues
risk_patterns.description: IP rights are territorial; governing law choice may not address registration, enforcement, and infringement in key jurisdictions.
risk_patterns.impact: Unenforceable IP provisions in key jurisdictions; gaps in territorial protection.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For IP-intensive international contracts, check if governing law adequately covers IP registration, enforcement, and infringement in all relevant territories.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include IP-specific jurisdiction provisions: registration territories, enforcement jurisdiction, and local counsel engagement for key markets.

risk_patterns.name: No End-of-Life Product Environmental Obligations
risk_patterns.trigger: Product supply contract without end-of-life environmental management obligations
risk_patterns.description: WEEE, battery regulations, and circular economy requirements impose take-back and recycling obligations.
risk_patterns.impact: Non-compliance with WEEE/battery regulations; disposal cost liability.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For electronic or battery product supply, check for WEEE compliance, battery regulation compliance, take-back obligations, and recycling targets.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include end-of-life clause: WEEE registration, battery regulation compliance, take-back scheme, and recycling rate targets.

risk_patterns.name: No Continuous Improvement Obligation
risk_patterns.trigger: Long-term service contract without continuous improvement and innovation obligations
risk_patterns.description: Without continuous improvement, service quality stagnates while market expectations advance.
risk_patterns.impact: Service quality falling behind market standards over multi-year terms.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For contracts >2 years, check for continuous improvement: annual improvement targets, innovation sharing, and technology refresh.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include continuous improvement: annual 3-5% efficiency targets, technology refresh obligations, innovation sharing, and benchmark comparison.

risk_patterns.name: No Reverse Break Fee in M&A
risk_patterns.trigger: M&A transaction with buyer walk-away right but no reverse break fee to protect seller
risk_patterns.description: Without reverse break fee, seller bears full deal costs and opportunity cost if buyer walks away.
risk_patterns.impact: Seller bears EUR 200K-5M in deal costs and lost alternative opportunities.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for reverse break fee: buyer failure to close triggers payment to seller, typically 3-5% of deal value.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include reverse break fee of 3-5% of deal value for buyer walk-away, triggered by failure to close by long-stop date without regulatory cause.

risk_patterns.name: No Data Ethics Framework for Analytics
risk_patterns.trigger: Advanced analytics or AI contract without data ethics framework and review process
risk_patterns.description: Legal compliance alone does not prevent ethical harms from data analytics; ethical framework provides additional safeguards.
risk_patterns.impact: Ethical harms from analytics creating reputational and legal exposure beyond GDPR compliance.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for data ethics provisions: ethics review process, prohibited uses, fairness assessment, and transparency commitment.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include data ethics framework: ethics review for high-impact analytics, prohibited use cases, fairness testing, and annual ethics audit.

risk_patterns.name: No Digital Asset Recovery Plan
risk_patterns.trigger: Contract without provisions for recovery of digital assets (domains, certificates, API keys, accounts)
risk_patterns.description: Digital assets controlled by service providers become inaccessible if not explicitly addressed in termination.
risk_patterns.impact: Loss of access to domains, certificates, cloud accounts, and API keys on provider termination.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for digital asset inventory and recovery: domains, SSL certificates, cloud accounts, API keys, social media, and code repositories.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include digital asset schedule: inventory of all assets, customer ownership documentation, transfer procedures, and 30-day recovery timeline.

risk_patterns.name: No AI Liability Directive Compliance
risk_patterns.trigger: AI system contract without provisions addressing EU AI Liability Directive requirements
risk_patterns.description: EU AI Liability Directive introduces presumption of causality and disclosure obligations for AI harm.
risk_patterns.impact: Reversed burden of proof for AI-caused harm; mandatory evidence disclosure obligations.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: Check for AI liability provisions: evidence preservation, disclosure obligations, causality documentation, and insurance coverage.
risk_patterns.remediation: Include AI liability clause: evidence preservation obligations, disclosure cooperation, incident documentation, and adequate liability insurance.

risk_patterns.name: No Source Code Escrow for Custom Software
risk_patterns.trigger: Custom software development contract without source code escrow for business continuity
risk_patterns.description: Without source code escrow, customer cannot maintain or modify software if developer ceases to operate.
risk_patterns.impact: Complete loss of ability to maintain business-critical custom software.
risk_patterns.detection_guidance: For custom software, check for source code escrow: independent agent, regular deposits, release triggers, and verification.
risk_patterns.remediation: Require source code escrow: independent agent, quarterly deposits with verification, release triggers (insolvency, material breach, cessation), and licence to use.
